title: Support MicroCharity
summary: All our administrative expenses are absorbed by MicroCharity volunteers. That gives us the unique tag of 'zero-expense charity' where every penny of the donor reaches the needy. Would you like to donate towards our running expenses? Please do!

updates.body:
All our administrative expenses are absorbed by MicroCharity volunteers. That gives us the unique tag of ’zero-expense charity’ where every penny of the donor reaches the needy. Would you like to donate towards our running expenses? Please do!

Your donation will be spend towards day to day running of MicroCharity like accouting expenses, IT expenses, even paying 2% commission towards each online transaction which we incur. All the works at MicroCharity is been done by its volunteers, so we dont have any salary expense!

Your help, however small it may be, will be really appreciated!

Next post"Cancer Treatment for Jesna"

updates.caption: Mar 10, 2019 - Changanassery, Kerala (MCID-132-18-19)
updates.body:
19 year old Jesna Emmanuel from Kerala has been suffering from cancer for the past 2 years. Doctors recommended a bone marrow transplant, but they have not been able to do it due to financial constraints.

So she is now going through chemotherapy, a course having 14 sessions at a cost of Rs. 75,000 for each session. Her father is working as a coolie and her mother is a homemaker. She also has a younger brother studying in school. At a monthly income of less than 10,000, the family has no means to continue Jesna’s treatment as they are already in debt from previous medical expenses. They are desperately in need of money now since Jesna is admitted at the hospital, since her blood count came down after chemotherapy.

updates.caption: Mar 13, 2019 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 75,000.

updates.caption: Mar 18, 2019 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund Raising closed for this cause. Rs. 75,000 is raised from 19 micro donations in 5 days. We will paying the amount directly to hospital shortly

updates.caption: Mar 23, 2019 - Aluva, Kerala (MCID-134-18-19)
updates.body:
36 year old Sreekala Shibu from Veliyathunad, Kerala is suffering from gallstones and is under treatment at Lissie hospital, Kochi. Doctors have advised cholecystectomy surgery to remove these gall stones. The family is not in a position to spend the Rs. 80,000 needed for the surgery which has to be done by 28th March. They have already spent Rs.40,000 at Amrutha hospital, sold gold and taken loan from Vincent De Paul for her treatment.

She lives with her husband, who is a painter and 2 children aged 9 and 2 years. They have 3 cents of land and a small house constructed under SC welfare department funding. Sreekala’s brother-in-law died of cancer a week back, and the family is shattered with grief and financial difficulties. Any money you can donate will go a long way to relieving the misery of this family.

updates.caption: Mar 25, 2019 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 50,000.

updates.caption: Mar 28, 2019 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising is closed for this cause. INR 50,000 raised from 18 micro donations in 3 days. The fund is transferred to hospital and the surgery is successfully completed.

title: Nursing School Fee for Ann Mary
summary: Ann Mary from Kannur, Kerala needs our financial support to start her Nursing degree. Her father is a rubber tapping worker earning less than Rs.4000 a month, mother is a house wife, elder sister is working as a nurse in Delhi and elder brother is doing his degree course.The family already has a loan of Rs. 4 lakh taken for her sister’s education, which she’s paying off from her salary. Her dad’s salary is not enough to sustain their household expenses along with Ann’s education. Total fee for 1st year BSc Nursing comes to Rs. 92,000 including tuition, hostel and food.

updates.caption: June 30, 2017 - Bangalore, Karnataka (MCID-116-17-18)
updates.body:
Ann Maria from Kannur, Kerala needs our financial support to start her Nursing degree. Her father is a rubber tapping worker earning less than Rs.4000 a month, mother is a house wife, elder sister is working as a nurse in Delhi and elder brother is doing his degree course.

The family already has a loan of Rs. 4 lakh taken for her sister’s education, which she’s paying off from her salary. Her dad’s salary is not enough to sustain their household expenses along with Ann’s education. Total fee for 1st year BSc Nursing comes to Rs. 92,000 including tuition, hostel and food.

updates.caption: September 5, 2017 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 50,000.

updates.caption: September 18, 2017 - Fund Raising closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. INR 50,000 raised from 10 micro donations in 13 days. Fund transferred directly to college.

updates.caption: Mar 18, 2019 - MicroCharity approves fund request (MCID-133-18-19)

Ann Maria completed her first year Nursing course with good marks. We continue to support her with INR 50,000 every year. The family, though they are very poor, have agreed to raise the rest of the fee themse lves.

MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 50,000.

Mar 28, 2019 – Fund raising closed for this cause. INR 50,000 is raised from 15 micro donations in 10 days. We have already transferred the fee to College directly.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 50,000.

Fund raising closed for this cause. Money will be transferred to college shortly.

title: House repairs for Mercy Michael
summary: Michael and Mercy Varghese from Changanassery in Kerala, need to repair their house urgently before the monsoons in Kerala. Aged 65 and 60, they are not able to work these days and are dependent on the government pension of Rs. 2500 per month. Michael was a daily wage worker earlier, but is not able to work anymore due to a heart condition.

updates.caption: April 1, 2019 - Changanassery, Kerala (MCID-101-19-20)
updates.body:
Michael and Mercy Varghese from Changanassery in Kerala, need to repair their house urgently before the monsoons in Kerala. Aged 65 and 60, they are not able to work these days and are dependent on the government pension of Rs. 2500 per month. Michael was a daily wage worker earlier, but is not able to work anymore due to a heart condition.

They are now staying in an old house that was built by the local church in 3 cents of land. The tin sheet roof is leaking and in bad condition. The house is also in need of urgent repairs. Some well-wishers came together to repair the house and build a western toilet for them considering their old age. The total cost of repairs would come to Rs. 150,000. They approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: April 8, 2019 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 50,000.

updates.caption: May 20, 2019 - Fud Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. Rs. 50,000 is raised from 13 micro donors in 42 days

title: Nursing School Fee for Dolapo
summary: Dolapo scored 75% marks for her 3rd year Nursing, and is in need of our support for the next year.MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 50,000.

updates.caption: February 18, 2018 - Bangalore, Karnataka (MCID-132-17-18)
updates.body:
Ojo Dolapo Ayomide from Nigeria is now doing her 3rd year Nursing course in Roohi College of Nursing, Bangalore. She needs our support to complete her education and support her family consisting of her father, mother, brother and sister back home. Dolapo came to Bangalore for her studies. She is staying at the college hostel and selling crochet handicrafts to earn money for her food and education.

Dolapo’s father Ojo Emmanuel Kolapo is not able to work anymore after he suffered a motor accident. Her mother designs and stiches garments earning a meager sum of 5000 NGN equivalent of Rs.900 per month. She is the only earning member of the family, and has to take care of the children’s education, and their family expenses. The family is finding it very difficult to support Dolapo’s education. Her fee for 3rd year Nursing comes to Rs. 180,000 to be paid as 2 instalments in March and September. Dolapo approached MicroCharity for financial assistance. MicroCharity offered to support her with 2 instalments of Rs. 50,000.

updates.caption: February 22, 2018 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 50, 000.

updates.caption: March 05, 2018 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. Rs. 50, 000 is raised from 13 micro donations in 11 days. We will be paying the fee directly to college shortly.

updates.caption: May 1, 2019 - Bangalore, Karnataka (MCID-102-19-20) - Fee for 4th year
updates.body:
Dolapo scored 75% marks for her 3rd year Nursing, and is in need of our support for the next year. Fee for the 4th year comes to Rs.160,000. She approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs.50,000.

updates.caption: May 30, 2019 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. Rs.50,000 is raised from 7 donors in 29 days. We will be paying the fee to college shortly.

title: Charity Initiatives – Junior Chamber International, Nalukody, Kerala

updates.caption: May 9, 2019 - Kerala, India (MCID-103-19-20)
updates.body:
MicroCharity is proud to join hands with JCI Nalukody, Kerala in their charitable initiatives. JCI would be referring causes which would be listed on the MicroCharity website. Donations would be collected from JCI members as well as donors at MicroCharity and transferred towards these causes. We look forward to a fruitful association with JCI in the coming years.

JCI ( Junior Chamber International) is the world’s leading network of young active citizens with presence across 100 countries as 5000 local organizations. JCI seeks to empower young citizens to become leaders of positive change. ‘Service to humanity is the best work of life’ is one of the tenets of JCI Creed, and all Jaycees give importance to value of giving and service to the society.

JCI Nalukody has been representing the spirit of Jaycees since 2010. JCI Nalukody has had its focus on the spirit of giving and has undertaken various charity projects such as Palliative care, Support of Institutions such as Thiruhrudhya Nivas for Autistic Children, providing technical and monetary support for reconstruction of homes of the underprivileged, financial support for education of underprivileged students, financial support for marriages and more.

We have also tried to become agents of positive change in our society by taking up and successfully executing ambitious projects such as Plastic recycling at Nalukody, pushing the agenda of developing Nalukody as a ‘SMART JUNCTION’. We have designed and installed modern bus waiting shelters at strategic points in Nalukody. Over and above, we have been actively supporting the Govt UP School Paippad and St Joseph’s Girls Higher Secondary school monetarily through infrastructure support, trainings, and scholarships.

JCI Nalukody is always on the lookout for opportunities to bring in positive change in the region around Nalukody in Kottayam District and to be an inspiration for other like-minded organisations.

You can read more about the causes referred by JCI on this page.

Please visit following pages for more details on specific causes under this initiative

Toilet Facility to Mercy Michael House – Rs 50,000
School Text Books to Mercy Home Orphanage – Rs 50,000
House Roof Maintenance to Kochurani – Rs 70,000
Medical Support to Joseph Mathew – Rs. 1 Lakh
School Uniform to Government School, Nalukody – Rs 27,500
Environmental – Tree Plantation Drive – Rs. 11,100

title: Surgery for Joseph Mathew
summary: Joseph Mathew, from Kottayam, Kerala is in the Neuro ICU Ventilator at Pushpagiri Hospital Tiruvalla, in a coma state due to bleeding in the brain. He was admitted to the hospital on 7th May with weakness on one side of the body and deviation in the angle of his mouth. Doctors diagnosed intracerebral hemorrhage and has recommended an emergency surgery to save his life. Joseph was working as a mechanic in a 2-wheeler repair shop and is the only earning member of the family. His wife is a home maker and he also has two young daughters aged 13 and 8. The family has already spent Rs. 4 lakhs for his treatment, and is in no position to pay for this surgery. They approached MicroCharity for financial support.

updates.caption: Oct 1, 2012 - Kochuthovala, Kattappana, Kerala (MCID-111-12)
updates.body: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 10 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. The family is unable to repay the loans as they have to spend on the treatment. She had a tumour in uterus and she had to go through a hysterectomy. Now she is also suffering from kidney stone problems.

updates.caption: Oct 16, 2012 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body:
Microcharity is supporting Nimal and her family by bearing a part of the medical expenses. Microcharity approved Rs. 10,000 for the year 2012. The plan is to raise Rs 10,000 for her in each quarter of the year and continuously support her in medical expenses.

Oct 18, 2012: MicroCharity raised Rs. 10,000 from 8 different individuals in 2 days. The fund is transferred to Nimal’s family.

updates.caption: January 29, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 1, 2013
updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well these days. She is now admitted at a hospital due to continuous nausea and vomiting. This is due to the hormone level issues she has been fighting with. The fund raised by MicroCharity was utilized to treat the hormone issue. She is also suffering from severe muscle strain. MicroCharity decided to continue supporting her by raising Rs. 10,000 for the first quarter of 2013.

updates.body:

title: School uniforms for children
summary: The Government UP School at Nalukody, Kerala has been providing education to children from financially backward and troubled families for the past 116 years. Though the school gets government aid, most of the infrastructure expenses need to be funded by the school itself. There is also a Kindergarten which is independent from the school, but is crucial since it serves as a feeder to the main school. This section does not get any government aid.Since the children are from poor families, the school has to provide everything from books, uniforms, bags, umbrellas and even their meals.

updates.caption: June 1, 2019 - Changanassery, Kerala (MCID-100-5-19-20)
updates.body:
The Government UP School at Nalukody, Kerala has been providing education to children from financially backward and troubled families for the past 116 years. Though the school gets government aid, most of the infrastructure expenses need to be funded by the school itself. There is also a Kindergarten which is independent from the school, but is crucial since it serves as a feeder to the main school. This section does not get any government aid.

 

Since the children are from poor families, the school has to provide everything from books, uniforms, bags, umbrellas and even their meals. The Headmaster of the school approached JCI Nalukody seeking support for school uniform for 30 new LKG and UKG students. JCI Nalukody in association with MicroCharity decided to sponsor 2 sets of uniform for these 30 children, along with annual subscription of newspaper and journals for the school.

The uniforms would cost about Rs.750 per child. Total cost for uniforms and subscriptions come to Rs.27,500

updates.caption: Jun 7, 2019 - Fund Request Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 27,500.

updates.caption: June 12, 2019 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund Raising closed for this cause. Rs.27,500 is raised

title: School fee for children of Mercy Home

updates.caption: May 14, 2019 - Changanassery, Kerala (MCID-100-2-19-20)
updates.body:
Mercy Home is run by Sisters of the Destitute (SD Sisters), housing 20 children between the ages 5 – 18 years. They belong to CWC – Child Welfare Commitee, Thiruvanthapuram registered under the Juvenile Justice Act. These children are studying at St Ann’s and St Joseph’s Girls High School, Changancherry. The home is not getting any grant from the government, and the sisters are currently unable to afford school expenses of these children. A group of well-wishers from the surrounding areas and from their congregation join hands to support them with about Rs. 25,000 per month. But this is barely enough for their regular expenses.

 

JCI Nalukody along with MicroCharity is extending their support for these needy children. Total fee for this academic year comes to Rs. 50, 000.

updates.caption: May 15, 2019 - Fund Request Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request for Rs. 50,000.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund Raising closed for this cause.

title: House repairs for Kochurani
summary: Ms. Kochurani Mathew, Nalukody, Changanacherry is the only breadwinner of the family consisting of Husband and 2 sons.Her husband met with multiple accidents over last 3 years after which he is unable to work. She earns her living by undertaking small tailoring works at her home which is not enough for her to maintain the family. The two sons are brilliant students studying in class 11th & 12th, both of them where school toppers in their respective class 10th.

updates.caption: June 3, 2019 - Changanassery, Kerala (MCID-100-6-19-20)
updates.body:
Kochurani Mathew from Changanacherry, Kerala needs our help in repairing their leaking house before the monsoons set in. She is staying with her husband and 2 sons, studying in 11th and 12th standards respectively. Her husband met with multiple accidents over the past 3 year and is not able to work anymore. Kochurani takes up small tailoring works at her home, but the income is barely enough to meet their daily expenses and children’s education.

 

As monsoons started in Kerala, their house started leaking badly to the point that they have to dry the school books in the sun after every rain. The family does not have any other place to move to. They approached JCI Nalukody for financial assistance in repairing the roof. On inspection, they found that the roof tiles were broken in multiple places. And the wooden frame holding the roof has been damaged with age. The frame needs to be replaced with a more durable steel frame and the roof tiles have to be relaid, replacing any broken ones. MicroCharity in association with JCI is taking up this repair work at a total expense of Rs. 70,000.

updates.caption: Jun 13, 2019 - Fund Request Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs.70,000.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause.

title: Tree Plantation Drive by JCI Nalukody
summary: “I realize that every mature tree by photosynthesis absorbs 20kg of CO2 every year. By the same process, each tree lets out about 14kg of O2 every year.”This is part of the environment oath taken by every member who planted a tree during this tree plantation drive. JCI Nalukody with support from Junior Jaycees, the children’s wing, organized a tree plantation drive on World Environment day. JCI took order from people for different fruit trees, by publishing on social media. Those who gave the order were supposed to have pits ready for planting trees in their compound. JCI members procured the saplings from nurseries and took them to these houses. They planted the trees together and also took an oath promoting awareness of environment protection and importance of trees.

updates.caption: June 3, 2019 - Changanassery, Kerala (MCID-100-6-19-20)
updates.body:
“I realize that every mature tree by photosynthesis absorbs 20kg of CO2 every year. By the same process, each tree lets out about 14kg of O2 every year.”

 

This is part of the environment oath taken by every member who planted a tree during this tree plantation drive. JCI Nalukody with support from Junior Jaycees, the children’s wing, organized a tree plantation drive on World Environment day. JCI took order from people for different fruit trees, by publishing on social media. Those who gave the order were supposed to have pits ready for planting trees in their compound. JCI members procured the saplings from nurseries and took them to these houses. They planted the trees together and also took an oath promoting awareness of environment protection and importance of trees.

 

Following the massive success of this program, JCI and MicroCharity are now extending this for the whole of June. For a contribution of Rs.222, a tree will the planted in your name in a house and will be taken care of by the owners. For those of you living in cities with no option to plant a tree in your backyard, this is a golden opportunity to make a difference to mother Earth on this Environment day.

updates.caption: Jun 15, 2019 - Fund Request Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs.11,100 for planting 50 trees.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause.

title: Medical Support for Jibin
summary: Jibin Joseph is a severe autistic baby with cerebral palsy by birth. He has no sense of hearing and is unable to speak. He walks a little.  He cannot feed himself.  He is studying in Adarsh Rehabilitation Centre, Tripunithura, Ernakulam - A Special school for the habilitation of children with Cerebral Palsy.  His mother takes him to the school everyday. They rely on public transport to commute to the school. Also, the mother has to be with him throughout the school time.  He is on medication for the last few years. Every month he is taken to a hospital for medical checkup.

updates.caption: Aug 15, 2012 - Kaduthuruthy, Kottayam, Kerala (MCID-107-12)
updates.body:
Jibin Joseph is a severe autistic baby with cerebral palsy by birth. He has no sense of hearing and is unable to speak. He walks a little.  He cannot feed himself.  He is studying in Adarsh Rehabilitation Centre, Tripunithura, Ernakulam – A Special school for the habilitation of children with Cerebral Palsy.  His mother takes him to the school everyday. They rely on public transport to commute to the school. Also, the mother has to be with him throughout the school time.  He is on medication for the last few years. Every month he is taken to a hospital for medical checkup.

His Father is a Salesman in an Optical shop nearby and mother is a housewife. His father cannot afford school and medical expenses for Jibin  from his Salary. Yearly fee in the Special school is Rs 13,750. School uniform, Bus fare, Books & sensory kit will cost another Rs 8,900. Yearly Medical expenses will be approximately another Rs 10,000.

updates.caption: Aug 22, 2012 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: Microcharity decided to take up the medical expenses for Jibin every year and approved the fund request of Rs. 10,000.  Deadline for raising the fund for this year is September 05, 2012.

updates.caption: Sep 01, 2012 - Rs. 10,000 raised from 10 Donors
updates.body: Rs. 10,000 raised from 10 Donors in about  8 days. The fund is transferred to Jibin in two installments on receipt of medical bills.

updates.caption: May 5, 2013 - Fund request for year 2013 (MCID-104-13-14)
updates.body: Jibin is attending his school regularly. The family is trying out ayurveda, homoeopathy and allopathy medicine to find where the child is responding. The yearly cost of medication is Rs. 35000. MicroCharity approved Rs. 10,000 for the the year 2013.

updates.caption: May 15, 2013 - Rs. 10,000 raised from 8 Donors
updates.body: Rs. 10,000 raised from 8 Donors in about  10 days.

updates.caption: May 25, 2014 - Fund request for year 2014 (MCID-106-14-15)
updates.body:
Jibin is doing good. The 14 year old is now taller than his mother. This is making it a little difficult for his mother to take him to the school everyday. So, he is attending the school only three days a week. Jibin’s eye coordination has improved much. He is now able to watch the television.

MicroCharity is continuing to support Jibin by raising Rs. 10,000 for year 2014.

updates.caption: May 31, 2014 - Rs. 10,000 raised from 7 Donors
updates.body: Rs. 10,000 raised from 7 Donors in about  5 days.

updates.caption: July 11, 2016 - Fund request for year 2016 (MCID-114-16-17)
updates.body:
We continue to support Jibin, whom we have been helping from August 2012. He is 16 years old now and continuing his studies at Adarsh Rehabilitation, Thrippunithura. And we are glad to say that he is showing slight improvement.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs.10,000.

updates.caption: Aug 05, 2016 - Fund raising completed.
updates.body: Rs. 10,000 raised from 8 Donors in about  10 days.

updates.caption: June 13, 2017 - Fund request for year 2017 (MCID-107-17-18)
updates.body:
Jibin is going back to Adarsh Rehabilitation center after summer holidays. Though he was down with fever, he has recovered now and is excited about the new academic year. This young child with cerebral palsy needs our constant support, and MicroCharity continues to support him every year since 2012 especially through the goodwill of our donors and well-wishers.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 10,000.

updates.caption: June 16, 2017 - Fund raising completed.
updates.body: Rs. 10,000 raised from 4 Donors in about 3 days.

updates.caption: June 26, 2018 - Kaduthuruthy, Kottayam, Kerala (MCID-113-18-19)
updates.body: MicroCharity has been supporting Jibin, suffering from cerebral palsy from when he was 12 years old. He is 18 now, and enjoys going to the rehabilitation center everyday. Let’s continue to support him and pray for his family.

updates.caption: June 30, 2018 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 10,000.

updates.caption: July 7, 2018 - Fund raising completed.
updates.body: Rs. 10,000 raised from 5 Donors in about 7 days. We will be paying this money towards Jibin’s medical/educational expenses.

updates.caption: June 16, 2019 - Kaduthuruthy, Kottayam, Kerala (MCID-104-19-20)
updates.body: MicroCharity continues to support Jibin, who has been affected with autism and cerebral palsy since birth. For the 7th year now, we are providing Rs.10,000 financial support for the family.

updates.caption: June 17, 2019 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 10,000.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund Raising closed for this cause. We will be transferring money to Jibin against Medical Expenses

updates.caption: Sep 19, 2020 - Kaduthuruthy, Kottayam, Kerala (MCID-109-20-21)
updates.body: Jibin, whom we have been supporting since 2012 is 20 years old now. He was born with severe autism and cerebral palsy. We continue to support his education, since his parents’ meager income is not enough to take care of this.

updates.caption: Oct 19, 2020 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 10,000.

updates.caption: Oct 22, 2020 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund Raising closed for this cause. We will be transferring money to Jibin against Medical Expenses

title: School fee for Jeraldina and Jeromina
summary: Twin sisters, Jeraldina and Jeromina’s story is not different from many lower income families in Bangalore, India. Their dad Sesu Raj, the sole bread winner of the family makes about Rs.200 per day working as an auto driver. But he is a chronic alcoholic and does not go to work regularly. The family is now struggling to get the kids to school. The little girls are in 1st standard and at a risk of not going to school at all, due to their financial difficulties. They have other loans to pay off and also a monthly rent of Rs.1500 for the house they are staying in.

updates.caption: May 10, 2017 - Bangalore, Karnataka (MCID-105-17-18)
updates.body:
Twin sisters, Jeraldina and Jeromina’s story is not different from many lower income families in Bangalore, India. Their dad Sesu Raj, the sole bread winner of the family makes about Rs.200 per day working as an auto driver. But he is a chronic alcoholic and does not go to work regularly. The family is now struggling to get the kids to school. The little girls are in 1st standard and at a risk of not going to school at all, due to their financial difficulties. They have other loans to pay off and also a monthly rent of Rs.1500 for the house they are staying in.

The fee for both children combined come to Rs.25,700 for this year. The family approached MicroCharity for assistance in paying the school fee.

updates.caption: May 30, 2017 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs.21,000.

updates.caption: June 1, 2017 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. Rs.21,000 is raised from 6 micro donations in 2 days. We will paying the fee shortly to school.

updates.caption: May 14, 2018 - Bangalore, Karnataka (MCID-104-18-19)
updates.body:
Jeraldina and Jeromina are doing really well in their studies. Jeraldina scored above 90% for all subjects, and Jeromina above 70%. Both are going to third standard this academic year. The family is still struggling to pay the school fee, and approached MicroCharity for financial support.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 25,000.

updates.caption: May 19, 2018 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. Rs.25,000 is raised from 5 micro donations in 5 days. We will paying the fee shortly to school.

updates.caption: July 3, 2019 - Bangalore, Karnataka (MCID-105-19-20)
updates.body:
Jeraldina and Jeromina are 2 diligent little girls, who will have to drop out of school without our support. They scored A or A+ for all subjects in 3rd grade and has been promoted to 4th. Their mother is still struggling to pay their school fee and approached MicroCharity for financial support.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 30,000.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. Rs.30,000 is raised and We will paying the fee shortly to school directly.

title: College Fee for Merlina
summary: MicroCharity has been supporting Merlina from 4th standard. In spite of many adverse condition like her father’s death, her mother’s inability to go to work, and living with her uncle who wanted them to move it, Merlina got decent marks in her 1st PUC examication. She’s in 2nd PUC now, and the family is in no position to pay the college fee.

updates.caption: July 1, 2011 - Jakkur, Bangalore
updates.body: Merlina Monica is a 10 year old girl studying in 4th standard. Her parents divorced 4 years back, and she is now living with her mother and grand mother. Her mother works as a cleaning lady, but earns only Rs. 2500 a month. She is asked to quit school as the fees is not paid in full. The school fee is Rs. 4500, out of which Rs. 1500 is already paid. She now requires Rs. 3000 for the school fee and Rs. 1000 for uniforms, books and shoes. There is a monthly school fee of Rs. 250 which the mother will take care. So the fund required is Rs. 4000.

updates.caption: July 5, 2011 - MicroCharity approves fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approved the fund request of Rs. 4000.

updates.caption: July 20, 2011 - Raised Rs. 4000 from 9 donors
updates.body: We raised Rs. 4000 from 9 donors. The amount is handed over to the mother of Merlina. She is back at school.

updates.caption: July 1, 2012 - Fund request for School fee
updates.body: Merlina is now studying in 5th standard. The families condition hasn’t improved much. Her father died recently due to excessive alcohol consumption. She requested Rs. 6,500 for school fee, uniform, and books.

updates.caption: July 13, 2012 - MicroCharity approves fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approved the fund request, and decided to raise Rs. 8,000, a little above the requested fund of Rs. 6,500. We hope the little extra money brings a little more happiness to the poor family.

updates.caption: July 22, 2012 - The payment and the surprise
updates.body: Merlina met us with her mom at Bangalore. She needed the money on July 23rd, the deadline given by the school authorities. We informed her mother that we would be paying Rs. 8,000, a little more than her fund request. To our surprise, she refused to take the little extra money saying ’it would help someone else in need’. The school fee is Rs. 6,175 and she wouldn’t take anything more than that. We were really moved by her response and paid her Rs. 6,175. The amount was raised from 4 donors.

updates.caption: Nov 14, 2012 - 1st assessment exam
updates.body: She got above average marks in the 1st   Assessment exam. We are motivating her to concentrate and study well.

updates.caption: June 10, 2013 - Fund request for School fee
updates.body: Merlina is now studying in 6th standard. She passed 5th standard with ’A grade’. She is a bit weak in mathematics and her mother is sending her to a tuition class nearby to improve her marks. This year she requested Rs. 7,500 against school fee, uniform, and books.

updates.caption: June 24, 2013 - MicroCharity approves fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approved the fund request, and decided to raise Rs. 7,500

updates.caption: June 26, 2013 - Raised Rs. 7500 from 6 donors
updates.body: Happy to announce that we  raised Rs. 7500 from 6 donors in 2 days. We will be paying the same towards her school fees.

updates.caption: July 02, 2013 - School Fees Paid
updates.body: School fee of Merlina is paid.

updates.caption: June 1, 2014 - Fund request for School fee - MCID-108-14-15
updates.body: Merlina has moved to 7th grade with good marks in all subjects, except Mathematics. Her mother is keen on continuing her education and approached MicroCharity for help with the tuition fee this academic year. MicroCharity team decided to collect a little extra this time since Merlina’s mother is suffering from backache and is not able to work every day.

updates.caption: June 5, 2014 - MicroCharity approves fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approved fund request of Rs.8,000

updates.caption: June 10, 2014 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund Raising is closed for this cause. Rs 8,000 is raised from 6 Micro donations in 5 days. We will paying this fee directly to school shortly.

updates.caption: July 5, 2015 - Fund request for School fee - MCID-112-15-16
updates.body: Merlina is slowly, but surely progressing in her studies and doing us proud. She scored 80% in all subjects in 7th grade and has moved to 8th grade now. MicroCharity has been supporting her from 4th standard and the family is very grateful for the help that you have given her. Merlina’s mother has continuous back pain which prevents her from doing regular jobs. She works is a daily wage sweeper in some offices nearby and the income is not enough to sustain her daughter’s education. School fee this year is Rs.10,000.

updates.caption: July 5, 2015 - MicroCharity approves fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approved fund request of Rs.10,000

updates.caption: June 9, 2015 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund Raising is closed for this cause. Rs 10,000 is raised from 5 Micro donations in 4 days. We will paying this fee directly to school shortly.

updates.caption: June 14, 2016 - Fund Request - School Fee for 2016- MCID-108-16-17
updates.body: Merlina is in 9th grade now, having scored 75% in her 8th grade exams.To add to the difficulties of the family, her mother met with an accident wherein she was hit by a bike. Her backache worsened as a result of this and she has not been able to go for work. Last month, she lost her job as a house maid, since she had to take too many days off. Merlina and her mother are staying with her uncle, who has also asked them to move out of their house since he cannot support them any longer. With all this adverse conditions, Merlina is still keen on studying and requested MicroCharity for support. We salute their spirit! Merlina’s school fee for this year is Rs.12,000 and we continue to support her through the generosity of our well-wishers.MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs.12,000.

updates.caption: June 16, 2016 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund Raising is closed for this cause. Rs 12,000 is raised from 4 Micro donations in 2 days. We will paying this fee directly to school shortly.

updates.caption: May 25, 2017 - Fund Request - School Fee for 2017 - MCID-104-17-18
updates.body: Merlina is in 10th grade now. Her mother is not able to work due to ill health, and is struggling to support her education. They approached MicroCharity for financial assistance this year as well. The school fee comes to Rs. 13,500.MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 13,500.May 29, 2017 – Fund Raising is closed for this cause. Rs 13,500 is raised from 6 Micro donations in 4 days. We will paying this fee directly to school shortly.

updates.caption: July 15, 2019 - Fund Request - School Fee for 2019 - MCID-106-19-20
updates.body:
MicroCharity has been supporting Merlina from 4th standard. In spite of many adverse condition like her father’s death, her mother’s inability to go to work, and living with her uncle who wanted them to move it, Merlina got decent marks in her 1st PUC examication. She’s in 2nd PUC now, and the family is in no position to pay the college fee.

Total fee for the year comes to Rs. 35,000 of which they can arrange Rs.15,000. The family requested MicroCharity to help with Rs. 20,000.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 20,000.

26-July-2019 : Fund raising is closed for this cause. Rs. 20,000 is raised from 8 micro donations in 11 days. We will be paying the fee to college shortly.

title: House Repair for Jainamma
summary: Jainamma Joseph from Changanassery, Kerala has to face the heavy Kerala monsoons under a leaky roof. She’s single and living all by herself. She is also offering services as a counsellor at a retreat center close to her house. After Jainamma recovered from a very serious illness, she decided to dedicate her life to the Lord. She is currently living in a small house in 4 cents of land, constructed with help from the Panchayat and well-wishers.

updates.caption: July 30, 2019 - Changanassery, Kerala - MCID-107-19-20
updates.body:
Jainamma Joseph from Changanassery, Kerala has to face the heavy Kerala monsoons under a leaky roof. She’s single and living all by herself. She is also offering services as a counsellor at a retreat center close to her house. After Jainamma recovered from a very serious illness, she decided to dedicate her life to the Lord. She is currently living in a small house in 4 cents of land, constructed with help from the Panchayat and well-wishers.

But the house is leaking badly during this time of the year, and she needs to do some urgent repairs. The proposed solution is to cover the entire roof with roofing sheets at an estimated cost of INR 65,000. Since Jainamma has no way to raise this money, she approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 65,000

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this form. Funds will be used shortly for repairing Jainamma’s house roof.

title: College Fee for Joseph
summary:
Joseph from Mundakkayam, Kerala is doing his BTech in Mechanical Engineering. He will be able to enter the final semesters of the course only if he remits the college fees on time.

His family is struggling financially as his father is under treatment for Kidney failure. The only income the family has at the moment is his mother's pension which is not enough to meet the expenses.

updates.caption: Aug 5, 2019 - Mundakkayam, Kerala - MCID-108-19-20
updates.body:
Joseph from Mundakkayam, Kerala is doing his BTech in Mechanical Engineering. He will be able to enter the final semesters of the course only if he remits the college fees on time.

His family is struggling financially as his father is under treatment for Kidney failure. The only income the family has at the moment is his mother’s pension which is not enough to meet the expenses.

Joseph has to complete his studies to support his family. He approached MicroCharity for financial support.

MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 90,000

updates.caption: Sep 2, 2019 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. Rs. 90,000 raised from 14 micro donations in 30 days.

title: House construction for Latha
summary: Latha and her three children, aged 18,14 and 13 have gone through more misfortunes than most of us can imagine. Latha was working as a kitchen assistant in a hospital. But she suffered severe burns from a kerosene stove explosion, resulting in her leg being amputated eight years back. She has not been able to work since then, and is still in need of medication and treatment about a decade after the mishap. She is also allergic to most medications making it extremely difficult to treat even minor illnesses. Latha’s husband was working a plantation. He was diagnosed with cancer in 2017 and passed away in February 2018.

updates.caption: Sep 1, 2019 - Wayanad, Kerala (MCID-110-19-20)
updates.body:
Latha and her three children, aged 18,14 and 13 have gone through more misfortunes than most of us can imagine. Latha was working as a kitchen assistant in a hospital. But she suffered severe burns from a kerosene stove explosion, resulting in her leg being amputated eight years back. She has not been able to work since then, and is still in need of medication and treatment about a decade after the mishap. She is also allergic to most medications making it extremely difficult to treat even minor illnesses.

Latha’s husband was working a plantation. He was diagnosed with cancer in 2017 and passed away in February 2018. Now there are no earning members in this family and their basic survival is in danger. Vismaya, the eldest daughter completed her 12th and joined CMA foundation course. She was recently taken to the doctor with severe bleeding and abnormal behavior. She was diagnosed with PCOS and severe depression, possibly the result of her seeing her dad go through cancer and death.

The family is surviving on support from NGOs, neighbours, well-wishers and the church. Annemarie foundation is paying their monthly rent, neighbours and the church is paying for groceries and basic expenses. With help from the block panchayat, 10 cents of land was purchased last year. But they have no money to start any construction. The family is completely broke and unable to build the house, provide medical treatment for Latha and Visamaya or even take care of their basic expenses.

They approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: Sep 4, 2019 - Fund Request Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs.1,00,000.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. 1 lakh is raised from 12 micro donations in 5 days.

updates.caption: Oct 1, 2012 - Kochuthovala, Kattappana, Kerala (MCID-111-12)
updates.body: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 10 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. The family is unable to repay the loans as they have to spend on the treatment. She had a tumour in uterus and she had to go through a hysterectomy. Now she is also suffering from kidney stone problems.

updates.caption: Oct 16, 2012 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body:
Microcharity is supporting Nimal and her family by bearing a part of the medical expenses. Microcharity approved Rs. 10,000 for the year 2012. The plan is to raise Rs 10,000 for her in each quarter of the year and continuously support her in medical expenses.

Oct 18, 2012: MicroCharity raised Rs. 10,000 from 8 different individuals in 2 days. The fund is transferred to Nimal’s family.

updates.caption: January 29, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 1, 2013
updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well these days. She is now admitted at a hospital due to continuous nausea and vomiting. This is due to the hormone level issues she has been fighting with. The fund raised by MicroCharity was utilized to treat the hormone issue. She is also suffering from severe muscle strain. MicroCharity decided to continue supporting her by raising Rs. 10,000 for the first quarter of 2013.

Feb 4, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 6 different donors in 6 days.

updates.caption: April 30, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 2, 2013
updates.body:
MicroCharity is continuing to support Nimal by raising Rs. 10,000 for the second quarter of 2013. Nimal is still not doing good and doctor’s have advised a thorough scan which will cost Rs. 25,000.

May 2, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 5 different donations in 3 days.

updates.caption: Aug 17, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 3, 2013
updates.body:
MicroCharity is continuing to support Nimal by raising Rs. 10,000 for the Third quarter of 2013. Nimal is still not doing good. Recently, her Calcium levels dropped very low and she is currently hospitalized at St Johns Hospital, Kattappana, Kerala .

Aug 21, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 10 different micro donations in 4 days.

updates.caption: Nov 13, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 4, 2013
updates.body:
MicroCharity is continuing to support Nimal by raising Rs. 10,000 for the fourth quarter of 2013.

Nov 15, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 6 different micro donations in 2 days.

updates.caption: Feb 1, 2014 - Fund raising for Quarter 1, 2014
updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well, and she was recently diagnosed with pituitary tumor and infection. In addition, she is also having problems due to Hiatal hernia. St. John’s Mission Hospital, Kattappana has sent her to Amrita Hospital for additional tests and check up.

The family is unable to keep up with all the medical expenses. MicroCharity approves an increased quarterly support amount of Rs. 50,000 this time, to help the family with the medical expenses.

Feb 6, 2014: Raised Rs. 50,000 from 23 micro donations in 3 days.

updates.caption: Apr 15, 2014 - Fund Raising for Quarter 2, 2014 (MCID-103-14-15)
updates.body:
Nimal continues to be sick and is being hospitalized frequently. MicroCharity is raising Rs. 10,000 for the second quarter of 2014 to help Nimal’s family with the medical expenses.

Apr 16, 2014: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 5 micro donations in 1 day.

updates.caption: July 23, 2014 - Fund Raising for Quarter 3, 2014 (MCID-118-14-15)
updates.body:
Nimal continues to be sick and is being hospitalized frequently. MicroCharity is raising Rs. 10,000 for the third quarter of 2014 to help Nimal’s family with the medical expenses.

Jul 31, 2014: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 5 micro donations in 7 days.

updates.caption: Oct 7, 2014 - Fund Raising for Quarter 4, 2014 (MCID-125-14-15)
updates.body:
Nimal is admitted at PVS Hospital, Ernakulam. MicroCharity is raising Rs. 10,000 for the fourth quarter of 2014 to help Nimal’s family with the medical expenses.

Oct 18, 2014: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 6 micro donations in 11 days.

updates.caption: Jan 12, 2015 - Fund Raising for Quarter 1, 2015 (MCID-133-14-15)

title: College fee for Harsha Thomas
summary: Harsha Thomas needs our financial support to complete her post-graduation course in MSc. Physics. She is the eldest of 6 children in a family that’s struggling to make ends meet, along with the education of the younger children. Thomas, her father is a daily wage worker and also does some farming to support his family.

updates.caption: Nov 10, 2018 - Mangalore, Karnataka (MCID-125-18-19)
updates.body:
Harsha Thomas needs our financial support to complete her post-graduation course in MSc. Physics. She is the eldest of 6 children in a family that’s struggling to make ends meet, along with the education of the younger children. Thomas, her father is a daily wage worker and also does some farming to support his family.

Harsha is studying at Alva’s college, Mangalore and her yearly fee is Rs. 75,000. Since family has no means of paying this, they approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

The yearly College fee alone is INR 63,000. He has other expenses and Hostel fee to pay.

title: Medical Treatment for Ramachandran
summary: Ramachandran, a 57-year-old paraplegic from Aluva, Kerala needs our support to complete payment for 2 previous surgeries and a third surgery that he needs urgently. He was a rubber-tapping worker. 19 years back he fell from a tree and was paralyzed waist-down. He is living with his wife, who does odd jobs when she is able to. Ramachandran’s son died in an accident some time back; his daughter is married and has 3 small children.

updates.caption: Nov 01, 2019 - Aluva, Kerala (MCID-114-19-20)
updates.body:
Ramachandran, a 57-year-old paraplegic from Aluva, Kerala needs our support to complete payment for 2 previous surgeries and a third surgery that he needs urgently. He was a rubber-tapping worker. 19 years back he fell from a tree and was paralyzed waist-down. He is living with his wife, who does odd jobs when she is able to. Ramachandran’s son died in an accident some time back; his daughter is married and has 3 small children.

He recently had to be admitted at Rajagiri Hospital, Aluva to undergo treatment and surgery for non-healing ulcers, urinary tract infection, decubitus ulcers and traumatic paraplegia. He underwent 2 surgeries, one on 12th August and the second one on the 19th of August. The total bill was 2.1 lakhs. Since he did not have money, he paid Rs. 35,000 and was discharged from the hospital with the promise that they would bring the money when he came for the next surgery.

The next surgery is also very important, and crucial to his healing. But the family is in no position to pay for this treatment. Total cost would be Rs. 3 lakhs including the pending amount to be paid for the previous surgeries. They have no source of income other than the money that Ramachandran’s wife earns from the small jobs that she gets.

In this desperate situation, they approached MicroCharity through a volunteer.

updates.caption: Nov 14, 2019 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: Microcharity approves INR 75,000 for Ramachandran’s treatment

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund Raising closed for this cause.

title: Medical Support for Nimal
summary: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 17 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. MicroCharity continues to support her every quarter since the past 7 years.

updates.caption: Oct 1, 2012 - Kochuthovala, Kattappana, Kerala (MCID-111-12)
updates.body: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 10 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. The family is unable to repay the loans as they have to spend on the treatment. She had a tumour in uterus and she had to go through a hysterectomy. Now she is also suffering from kidney stone problems.

updates.caption: Oct 16, 2012 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body:
Microcharity is supporting Nimal and her family by bearing a part of the medical expenses. Microcharity approved Rs. 10,000 for the year 2012. The plan is to raise Rs 10,000 for her in each quarter of the year and continuously support her in medical expenses.

Oct 18, 2012: MicroCharity raised Rs. 10,000 from 8 different individuals in 2 days. The fund is transferred to Nimal’s family.

MicroCharity decided to continue supporting her by raising Rs. 10,000 for the first quarter of 2013.

title: Kidney Transplantation for Amil
summary:
14-year-old Amil has more worries and pain in life than any normal teenager can imagine. He is suffering from kidney failure and both his kidneys have been removed. Till the doctors can complete an urgent kidney transplantation, he must undergo dialysis every day at a cost of Rs.6000 per day. He’s currently in the ICU at Medical Trust Hospital, Ernakulam.

Amil had problems with his urinary bladder from birth and was undergoing regular scan and treatment. But 6 months ago, he had a sudden infection and both kidneys failed. Doctors have recommended a kidney transplantation at the earliest at a cost of Rs. 8 lakhs. His mother is the donor. The surgery was scheduled for 31st of December, 2019, but they could not do it since Amil got high temperature. Once he’s better, the surgery will be scheduled again. His family is not in a position to meet these hefty medical expenses, and approached MicroCharity through our volunteers.

updates.caption: Dec 19, 2019 - Ernakulam, Kerala (MCID-116-19-20)
updates.body:
14-year-old Amil has more worries and pain in life than any normal teenager can imagine. He is suffering from kidney failure and both his kidneys have been removed. Till the doctors can complete an urgent kidney transplantation, he must undergo dialysis every day at a cost of Rs.6000 per day. He’s currently in the ICU at Medical Trust Hospital, Ernakulam.

Amil had problems with his urinary bladder from birth and was undergoing regular scan and treatment. But 6 months ago, he had a sudden infection and both kidneys failed. Doctors have recommended a kidney transplantation at the earliest at a cost of Rs. 8 lakhs. His mother is the donor. The surgery was scheduled for 31st of December, 2019, but they could not do it since Amil got high temperature. Once he’s better, the surgery will be scheduled again. His family is not in a position to meet these hefty medical expenses, and approached MicroCharity through our volunteers.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund Raising has been closed for this cause.

title: College fee for Martina
summary: Martina completed her PUC and has joined B Com. Her father is still not able to look after the family, and her mother's meager income is not sufficient to fund her college fee.

updates.caption: Jan 4, 2020 - Bangalore, Karnataka(MCID-117-19-20)
updates.body:
Martina from Bangalore, India needs our help in completing her PU course. Her father is a drunkard who does not look at the family. He comes home drunk late at night most days, causing a lot of nuisance to the family. Most of the days, they all have to go and seek shelter at a convent close by to escape the violence at home. Her mother, Elezabaeth is working as a helper at a factory nearby earning Rs. 6000 per month.

Martina completed her 1st year PU with help from the nuns at the convent, and few other well-wishers. The nuns also advised her to move into a hostel so that she would not be troubled by her father’s behavior. Course fee for second year PU is Rs.25,000 and hostel fee comes to Rs. 40,000. The family approached MicroCharity for financial assistance since they are not able to afford the college fee.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund Raising has been closed for this cause.

title: Hostel fee for Nikitha
summary: Nikitha from Malappuram, Kerala will not be able to complete her graduation course, without our help. She is doing BSc. CSME at Kristu Jayanti college, Bangalore. Her father Shajan is suffering from kidney failure and needs dialysis every week. Her mother, Laly is working as an accountant at Rs. 10,000 salary per month, which is the only source of income for their family.

updates.caption: Jan 7, 2020 - Malappuram, Kerala (MCID-118-19-20)
updates.body:
Nikitha from Malappuram, Kerala will not be able to complete her graduation course, without our help. She is doing BSc. CSME at Kristu Jayanti college, Bangalore. Her father Shajan is suffering from kidney failure and needs dialysis every week. Her mother, Laly is working as an accountant at Rs. 10,000 salary per month, which is the only source of income for their family.

They have already spent a lot of money for Shajan’s treatment and family is now struggling with education fee for Nikitha and her younger brother, who is still in school. The college fee of Rs. 30,000 has already been paid. But hostel fee of Rs. 15,000 and exam fee of Rs. 4,500 is still pending. The family has been staying in a rented house for the past 18 years and is struggling with their expenses. Hence, they approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund Raising has been closed for this cause.

title: Nursing College Fee for Shalat
summary: Shalat, daughter of Shaji and Shanty from Pathanamthitta, Kerala will have to discontinue her Nursing course without our financial support. Her father Shaji is a farmer who is not able to work these days since he has a steel rod inserted in his legs post a recent accident. Her mother is a home maker and younger sister is still in school. Her father Shaji is a farmer who is not able to work these days since he has a steel rod inserted in his legs post a recent accident. Her mother is a home maker and younger sister is still in school. The family is struggling to meet their daily expenses.

Shalat got admissions in a private college where the total fee for first year is 97,500. They have already paid 31,000 and need our help in paying the rest. They approached MicroCharity for financial support.

Shalat was stuggling with studies during the initial months since she had studied in a Malayalam-medium school, and the language switch was tough. But she is coping well now, and very keen to continue her studies.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 77,500

title: Help Shanil get back to Business!
summary:
In the early hours of Sunday, March 1, a major fire incident affected the office of Archikites in Payyannur, a Website Design and training company run by Shanil who is a member Vijayee Bhava Batch (VB9).

Hopefully, no one was hurt. But, the fire destroyed the entire 2200 square feet space. Shanil lost about 25 computers, other office equipments, and the interior of the office is damaged beyond repair. The net loss is estimated to be about 25 lakh rupees.

updates.caption: Mar 5, 2020 - Payyannur, Kerala (MCID-122-19-20)
updates.body:
In the early hours of Sunday, March 1, a major fire incident affected the office of Archikites in Payyannur, a Website Design and training company run by Shanil, a member of Vijayee Bhava Batch 9 (VB9).

Hopefully, no one was hurt. But, the fire destroyed the entire 2200 square feet space. Shanil lost about 25 computers, other office equipments, and the interior of the office is damaged beyond repair. The net loss is estimated to be about 25 lakh rupees.

As fellow VBians, it is our responsibility to support Shanil in this difficult time. He is determined to fight back, and we will support him to get back on his feet as soon as he can. His batch mates from VB9 are determined to raise at least 5 lakh rupees to support Shanil get back to business. 

Inviting every VBian to be a part of this noble cause! Vijayee Bhava!

The fund raising is organized through MicroCharity, a zero profit charity organization, who doesn’t charge any fees on fund raising.

title: Medical Support for Nimal – June 2020
summary: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 18 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. The family is unable to repay the loans as they have to spend on the treatment. She had a tumour in uterus and she had to go through a hysterectomy. Now she is also suffering from kidney stone problems.

title: Textile Shop for Sheeja
summary:
Sheeja, a 35-year-old lady from Pathanamthitta, Kerala has more worries on her plate than she can take on by herself. Her daughter, Karthika passed away in December 2019 after a long battle with brain tumor. Medical expenses required the
family to take multiple loans from banks and from other sources, but the little girl could not be saved. Sheeja is now living with her 11-year-old son, Karthik and her aged mother-in-law. Sheeja used to do some tailoring at home, and also supply snacks to neighbouring homes. But there is no fixed income, and the family is now struggling to take care of their expenses. They are staying in a rented house and paying the rent with help from well-wishers. She has a housing loan of 6 lakhs, a Rs. 50,000 loan from the society and also a few personal loans.Her son, Karthik is not well. Sheeja herself underwent a heart surgery some time back.

updates.caption: June 1, 2020 - Pathanamthitta, Kerala (MCID-101-20-21)
updates.body:
Sheeja, a 35-year-old lady from Pathanamthitta, Kerala has more worries on her plate than she can take on by herself. Her daughter, Karthika passed away in December 2019 after a long battle with brain tumor. Medical expenses required the

family to take multiple loans from banks and from other sources, but the little girl could not be saved. Sheeja is now living with her 11-year-old son, Karthik and her aged mother-in-law. Sheeja used to do some tailoring at home, and also supply snacks to neighbouring homes. But there is no fixed income, and the family is now struggling to take care of their expenses. They are staying in a rented house and paying the rent with help from well-wishers. She has a housing loan of 6 lakhs, a Rs. 50,000 loan from the society and also a few personal loans.Her son, Karthik is not well. Sheeja herself underwent a heart surgery some time back. But to take care of their expenses, she now wants to start a textile shop. They have already shortlisted a shop for Rs. 10,000 rent per month. She also needs to get some cloth materials for Rs. 40,000. This family desperately needs our help to stand on their feet after their massive loss.

updates.caption: Jun 4, 2020 - Fund Request Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs.50,000.

updates.caption: June 11, 2020 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. Rs 50,000 is raised from 9 micro donations in 7 days. We will be handing over the money to sheeja for setting up her small cloth shop

title: Kidney Transplant for Josmy
summary:
26-year old Josmy has been suffering from kidney failure for over 7 years. She had a kidney transplant at the age of 17, her mom being the donor. But after 3 years, the donated kidney failed. She has been undergoing dialysis thrice a week for the past few years while searching for another suitable donor.

Currently, they found a donor, the crossmatch has been successful and other tests are going on for the transplant scheduled for next month. Josmy was a bright student, a fighter, and an optimist but years of treatment has made her very weak. Despite this, she faces the situation with a smile. She is from a very poor family and her father is a rickshaw driver. He has been finding money for all the dialysis, medications, and tests for several years with the help of a lot of kind souls.

Josmy is now undergoing treatment at ASTER MIMS, KOZHIKODE, KERALA. The cost of the transplant and post-transplant treatment is around 12 lakhs. For a family that is struggling to find money for their day-to-day expenses, this amount is beyond what they can manage. So they reached out to MicroCharity for financial assistance.

title: Medical Treatment for Joy
summary: 54-year-old Joy Antappan from Idukki, Kerala has been suffering from mental health issues for the past one year, following his father’s death. 3 months back, he had to undergo a major surgery to remove a growth from his stomach. Joy also has some heart-related complications. After the surgery, his mental health deteriorated, and he had to be admitted to a mental hospital near his house. The family is not able to keep him at home due to suicidal tendencies and violence.

updates.caption: Aug 1, 2020 - Idukki, Kerala (MCID-103-20-21)
updates.body:
54-year-old Joy Antappan from Idukki, Kerala has been suffering from mental health issues for the past one year, following his father’s death. 3 months back, he had to undergo a major surgery to remove a growth from his stomach. Joy also has some heart-related complications. After the surgery, his mental health deteriorated, and he had to be admitted to a mental hospital near his house. The family is not able to keep him at home due to suicidal tendencies and violence.

Joy was working as a daily wage laborer before he fell ill. His wife was also working as a daily wage laborer; but she had to discontinue work after a leg injury. Their daughter is married, and son is working at a mechanic shop close by.

The family has 10 cents of land and some support for house construction from the Panchayat. But Joy’s medical expense of around Rs. 30,000 per month is too much for the family to bear. They are not able to finish the construction or meet their daily expenses. The family is running from pillar to post to find money for his treatment, and they approached MicroCharity for financial support.

updates.caption: Aug 18, 2020 - Fund Request Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs.31,500.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. Rs 31,500 is raised from 6 micro donations in 2 days. We will be transferring money to hospital shortly

title: Dialysis charges for Rosamma
summary:
58-year old Rosamma Antony has been struggling with her dialysis expenses for the past 10 months. She and her husband were running a tea shop before she was diagnosed with kidney failure. She needs three dialysis per week at a cost of around Rs. 9000 per week. They had to close the tea shop since Rosamma must go to the hospital very frequently. She is also currently suffering from respiratory issues. The couple has 2 daughters who are already married but are in no position to support them.
The family has no other means of income and no way to raise the Rs. 36,000 needed each month for medical treatment. So they approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

title: Cancer treatment for Rajan
summary:
62-year old Rajan and his family is in dire need of our assistance. He was diagnosed with stomach cancer 2.5 years ago. He was treated at Amrita medical college where he had to spend a lot of money. He then went to Cochin Cancer research center at Kalamassery for chemotherapy and other treatments. The current treatment involves one injection per month for year, each costing Rs. 25,000 at a total of Rs. 3 lakhs.

Rajan was working as a manager in a hotel in Delhi. He came back to Kerala three years back and was staying in a rented house at Aluva. During the flood of 2018, he lost all his possessions. The family then shifted to a rented apartment at Kakkanad. Rajan’s wife is ill and cannot go for work. His daughter completed 12th grade and is working in a call center now. The only income for this family is his daughter’s salary, which is barely enough to cover their rent and daily expenses. The family is struggling to manage the injection costs and they reached out to MicroCharity for financial assistance.

title: Nursing college fee for Athulya Benny

Her mother Jessy has many health issues and the family had to spend a lot of money for her treatment. She also has a younger brother who is in school now. of the amount. The family has other loans, and also has to manage the continuous medical expenses for her mother. They approached MicroCharity for financial support in paying the college fee.

title: Medical Support for Nimal – September 2020
summary: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 18 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. MicroCharity continues to support Nimal for last 8 years.The family is struggling with her medical expenses and frequent hospitalizations.

updates.caption: Oct 1, 2012 - Kochuthovala, Kattappana, Kerala (MCID-111-12)

Now she is also suffering from kidney stone problems.

title: Liver Transplant Surgery for Roby
summary:
Roby, a 45-year old young man was working as a collection agent in a reputed bank, when tragedy struck in the form of liver disease. He has been suffering from chronic liver disease with portal hypertension for the past 2 years, and has to undergo liver transplantation at the earliest. The hospital has estimated Rs. 40 lakhs including surgery and post-surgical follow up.

Roby is living with his mother, aged 81, a pensioner; and his wife, who is working as a lab technician in a hospital nearby. He has 2 young children aged 14 and 9 years. The family has no means of raising Rs. 40 lakhs required for treatment. They collected Rs. 15.15 lakhs through a public campaign, of which Rs. 10 lakhs is already deposited at the hospital as advance. They approached MicroCharity through our volunteers, for financial support.

updates.body:

title: Brain Surgery complication – Albin
summary: 30-year-old Albin is going through a very tough time in his life and needs our financial support with prayers to help him return to normal life. He was diagnosed with colloid cyst in the brain, in August 2020 and underwent craniotomy for excision of cyst on the 12th of September. He was discharged on 19th September but was readmitted on the 20th with seizures.

updates.caption: Oct 15, 2020 - Kunnoth, Kannur, Kerala (MCID-110-20-21)
updates.body:
30-year-old Albin is going through a very tough time in his life and needs our financial support with prayers to help him return to normal life. He was diagnosed with colloid cyst in the brain, in August 2020 and underwent craniotomy for excision of cyst on the 12th of September. He was discharged on 19th September but was readmitted on the 20th with seizures.

CT scan revealed multiple hematomas in the brain and meningitis. He was shifted to Aster Mims hospital, Kozhikode, where he was diagnosed with a brain aneurysm. He underwent one more surgery for coiling of aneurysm and insertion of Ommaya reservoir.

He is currently in the Neurosurgical ICU with oxygen support through a tracheostomy tube. The family has already spent close to 12 lakhs for two surgeries and post-operative treatment. Albin will need a prolonged stay in the hospital before he is stabilized.

Albin currently stays with this aged father, who is working as a salesman; his mother who is not employed, his brother who is a student. He got married a couple of months back, and his newlywed wife is also staying with him. The family has no means to support Albin’s continued treatment. They approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: Oct 22, 2020 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 1,00,000.

updates.caption: Oct 22, 2020 - Albin is no more
updates.body: We have a sad news to update. Albin passed away this morning at MIMS Hospital, Kozhikode, Kerala. May his soul rest in peace.  May God give his family and his newly married wife the strength to cope with this massive loss. We will be transferring Rs. 25,000 which we raised so far to the family

title: Surgery for Thomas Joseph
summary:
45-year-old Thomas is a hard worker, earning and supporting his family, working as a coconut picker. On the 26th of October, 2020 he fell from a coconut tree and was badly injured. Thomas was rushed to the hospital and they found that his thigh bone had two fractures.

One surgery was completed urgently, and he is recovering. After observing him, they will decide whether he would need another surgery.

Thomas stays with his wife and 2 children, Neethu and Nihil who are still in school. He was the sole breadwinner for their family, and they have no other source of income. Recently, they also lost the money they had paid as advance for a house since the deed was not proper. The family is now struggling to meet hospital expenses.

The cost of surgery and treatment comes to Rs. 1,50,000. The family has arranged for Rs. 40,000 from friends and well-wishers, but that is not nearly enough to complete Thomas’ treatment. They approached MicroCharity for financial support.

title: Kidney surgery for Alan
summary: 11-year old has been suffering from vomiting and stomach pain for the past 6 months. He was taken to a private hospital where they diagnosed a block in the blood vessels of the kidney and recommended immediate surgery. Since the family is very poor, they took him to a government hospital, where he has been under treatment for the past 4 months.

updates.caption: Dec 1, 2020 - Thekkemala, Idukki (MCID-113-20-21)
updates.body:
11-year old has been suffering from vomiting and stomach pain for the past 6 months. He was taken to a private hospital where they diagnosed a block in the blood vessels of the kidney and recommended immediate surgery. Since the family is very poor, they took him to a government hospital, where he has been under treatment for the past 4 months.

Recently he developed an infection in the kidney, and they were draining the pus using an external tube inserted through his stomach. A few days back, the doctors at government hospital said that the right kidney needs to be removed urgently. When some relatives of Alan showed his reports at a hospital in Ernakulam, they said that the condition would be corrected with a surgery to remove the block.

There is a possibility that the kidney would not need to be removed. The parents were overjoyed at this, but they do not have enough money to pay for the surgery, which would cost about Rs. 3 lakhs. Alan is going through a harrowing 5-hour surgery as I am writing this at 11 am on 3rd December. The family does not have any way to pay the hospital bill. Friends and well-wishers are chipping in with small amounts since Alan’s dad is a daily wage laborer who has no way to fund this surgery.

They approached MicroCharity for financial assistance. Since this is a critical cause, we request your help in closing the fund collection as early as possible, so that Alan’s family can worry about their son’s recovery, and not about the money to be paid.

updates.caption: December 03, 2020 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 1 lakh.

updates.caption: December 06, 2017 - Fund Raising closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. Raised fund is transferred to hospital on December 04th, 2020. Alan got discharged after surgery and at a relatives home for a next 3 months with medical care.

updates.body:
