updates.caption: June 01, 2020 - Fund Raising for Quarter 3, 2020 (MCID-100-20-21)
updates.body:
Nimal has been paralyzed waist down for the past 18 years, as a result of a wrong injection administered to her in childhood. She has gone through a lot of hospitalizations and medical procedures; and her family is still struggling to fund her treatment. MicroCharity has been supporting her with Rs.10,000 every quarter or Rs. 40,000 every year. The family has always been extremely grateful for our support, and has never asked for more. But MicroCharity team felt that it would be good to increase the amount considering their increased expenses, to Rs.15,000 a quarter. Requesting all donors to please stand with us in this decision to increase our donation amount.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 15,000.

June 10,2020 – Fund raising closed for this cause. We have disbursed the amount to Nimal against her medical bills.

updates.caption: Sep 22, 2020 - Fund Raising for Quarter 4, 2020 (MCID-107-20-21)
updates.body:
MicroCharity continues to support Nimal, who has been paralyzed and bed-ridden for the past 18 years. The family is struggling with her medical expenses and frequent hospitalizations.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 15,000

Update : Fundraising closed for this cause. We will be disbursing money shortly to Nimal against here medical expenses.

updates.caption: Dec 07, 2020 - Fund Raising for Quarter 1, 2021 (MCID-114-20-21)
updates.body:
Nimal truly is a wonder, the way she has accepted her pain and sufferings over the last 18 years with maturity way beyond her years. She believes that God is with her, and with His strength, she stays strong and smiles through all her hardships.

MicroCharity is honored to continue supporting Nimal, who has been bedridden for the past 18 years. Listen to her story here, https://youtu.be/ZxetuOF6QPM (Malayalam video)

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 15,000

Update : Fundraising closed for this cause. We will be disbursing money shortly to Nimal against her medical expenses.

title: 2nd Year College fee for Deepu
summary: Deepu, an ambitious young man from Vandanmedu, Idukki needs our support to continue his studies. Deepu’s mother Sathi is a cook at a school near their house and earns Rs. 3,000 per month. That’s not nearly enough to meet the family’s daily expenses along with her children’s education. Deepu managed raise some money for his first year MCA course, but this year, he does not have any means to pay the 2nd year college fee of Rs. 60,500. MicroCharity continues to support Deepu in paying the fee for his MCA course.

updates.caption: Nov 01, 2019 - Idukki, Kerala (MCID-113-19-20)
updates.body:
Deepu, an ambitious young man from Vandanmedu, Idukki needs our support to continue his studies. He secured an admission for MCA course by working hard and with support from well wishers. He wants to complete the course to support his family.

Deepu lost his father when he was young. His mother’s small job is not sufficient to support the family’s needs. He has a younger brother doing his degree course.

The yearly College fee alone is INR 63,000. He has other expenses and Hostel fee to pay.

updates.caption: Nov 04, 2019 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: Microcharity approves INR 27,000 for the first year as rest of the money is supported by other well wishers this year.

updates.caption: Nov 15, 2019 Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund Raising closed for this cause. Amount is paid directly to college towards fee.

updates.caption: 2nd year Fee for Deepu - Jan 06, 2021 - Idukki, Kerala (MCID-115-20-21)
updates.body:
Deepu’s mother Sathi is a cook at a school near their house and earns Rs. 3,000 per month. That’s not nearly enough to meet the family’s daily expenses along with her children’s education. Deepu managed raise some money for his first year MCA course, but this year, he does not have any means to pay the 2nd year college fee of Rs. 60,500. MicroCharity continues to support Deepu in paying the fee for his MCA course.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 60,500.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund Raising closed for this cause. Amount will be paid directly to college towards fee.

title: ITI Course fee for Bibin
summary: Bibin Mathew needs our help in completing his ITI Electrical course. His father is an auto-driver who also does some farming in their own land. He is not able to work regularly due to his ill-health, and earn only about Rs.5000 per month. His mother is a homemaker, and his sister is still in school. The family has no means to pay the ITI college fee of Rs.35,000 from the meager income of his father.

updates.caption: Jan 20, 2021 - Paiskkary, Kannur, Kerala (MCID-116-20-21)
updates.body:
Bibin Mathew needs our help in completing his ITI Electrical course. His father is an auto-driver who also does some farming in their own land. He is not able to work regularly due to his ill-health, and earn only about Rs.5000 per month. His mother is a homemaker, and his sister is still in school. The family has no means to pay the ITI college fee of Rs.35,000 from the meager income of his father.

Bibin managed to collect Rs.5000 from his friends, but he is still short on money and struggling to fulfill his dreams of studying further. He approached MicroCharity for financial support through a well-wisher.

updates.caption: Jan 25, 2021 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request request of Rs.30,000.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund Raising closed for this cause. Amount will be paid directly to college towards course fee.

title: College fee for Somina
summary: 19-year-old Somina needs our help in starting her Medical Imaging course. Her father is a daily wage laborer and her mother is a home maker. She has a brother and sister who are still in school. Somina’s father earns Rs.20,000 per month and is not able to sustain their daily household expenses plus children’s education.

updates.caption: Jan 25, 2021 - Kasaragod, Kerala (MCID-117-20-21)
updates.body:
19-year-old Somina needs our help in starting her Medical Imaging course. Her father is a daily wage laborer and her mother is a home maker. She has a brother and sister who are still in school. Somina’s father earns Rs.20,000 per month and is not able to sustain their daily household expenses plus children’s education.

They have taken an education loan for Rs. 40,000. But that’s not nearly enough to pay the college fee of Rs. 1,01,600 for Somina’s vocational course in Medical Imaging Technology (B-Voc MIT). So she approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: Feb 3, 2021 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 13,500 towards first year College Fee.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Somina’s college.

title: B.Com Fees for Martina
summary: Martina completed her PUC and has joined BCom. Her father is still not able to provide for the family, and her mother's meager income is not sufficient to fund her college fee. She needs our support to continue her studies.

updates.caption: Feb 20, 2021 - Bangalore, Karnataka(MCID-118-20-21)
updates.body:
Martina Martina completed her PUC and has joined BCom. Her father is still not able to look after the family, and her mother’s meager income is not sufficient to fund her college fee.

College fee for 1st year BCom is INR 35000, of which MicroCharity is sponsoring INR 25,000.

updates.caption: Feb 20, 2021 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 25000.

updates.caption: Jan 4, 2020 - Bangalore, Karnataka(MCID-117-19-20)
updates.body:
Martina from Bangalore, India needs our help in completing her PU course. Her father is a drunkard who does not look after the family. He comes home drunk late at night most days, causing a lot of nuisance to the family. Most of the days, they all have to go and seek shelter at a convent close by to escape the violence at home. Her mother, Elizabeth is working as a helper at a factory nearby earning INR 6000 per month.

Martina completed her 1st year PU with help from the nuns at the convent, and few other well-wishers. The nuns also advised her to move into a hostel so that she would not be troubled by her father’s behavior. Course fee for second year PU is INR 25,000 and hostel fee comes to INR 40,000. The family approached MicroCharity for financial assistance since they are not able to afford the college fee.

updates.caption: Fund raising closed
updates.body: Fund raising is closed. The fund will be transferred to Martina’s college.

title: Medical Support for Nimal – March 2021
summary: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 18 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. MicroCharity continues to support Nimal for last 8 years.The family is struggling with her medical expenses and frequent hospitalizations.

updates.caption: Oct 1, 2012 - Kochuthovala, Kattappana, Kerala (MCID-111-12)
updates.body: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 10 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. The family is unable to repay the loans as they have to spend on the treatment. She had a tumour in uterus and she had to go through a hysterectomy. Now she is also suffering from kidney stone problems.

updates.caption: Oct 16, 2012 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body:
Microcharity is supporting Nimal and her family by bearing a part of the medical expenses. Microcharity approved Rs. 10,000 for the year 2012. The plan is to raise Rs 10,000 for her in each quarter of the year and continuously support her in medical expenses.

Oct 18, 2012: MicroCharity raised Rs. 10,000 from 8 different individuals in 2 days. The fund is transferred to Nimal’s family.

updates.caption: January 29, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 1, 2013
updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well these days. She is now admitted at a hospital due to continuous nausea and vomiting. This is due to the hormone level issues she has been fighting with. The fund raised by MicroCharity was utilized to treat the hormone issue. She is also suffering from severe muscle strain. MicroCharity decided to continue supporting her by raising Rs. 10,000 for the first quarter of 2013.

Feb 4, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 6 different donors in 6 days.

updates.caption: April 30, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 2, 2013
updates.body:
MicroCharity is continuing to support Nimal by raising Rs. 10,000 for the second quarter of 2013. Nimal is still not doing good and doctor’s have advised a thorough scan which will cost Rs. 25,000.

May 2, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 5 different donations in 3 days.

updates.caption: Aug 17, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 3, 2013
updates.body:
MicroCharity is continuing to support Nimal by raising Rs. 10,000 for the Third quarter of 2013. Nimal is still not doing good. Recently, her Calcium levels dropped very low and she is currently hospitalized at St Johns Hospital, Kattappana, Kerala .

Aug 21, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 10 different micro donations in 4 days.

updates.caption: Nov 13, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 4, 2013
updates.body:
MicroCharity is continuing to support Nimal by raising Rs. 10,000 for the fourth quarter of 2013.

Nov 15, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 6 different micro donations in 2 days.

updates.caption: Feb 1, 2014 - Fund raising for Quarter 1, 2014
updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well, and she was recently diagnosed with pituitary tumor and infection. In addition, she is also having problems due to Hiatal hernia. St. John’s Mission Hospital, Kattappana has sent her to Amrita Hospital for additional tests and check up.

The family is unable to keep up with all the medical expenses. MicroCharity approves an increased quarterly support amount of Rs. 50,000 this time, to help the family with the medical expenses.

Feb 6, 2014: Raised Rs. 50,000 from 23 micro donations in 3 days.

updates.caption: Apr 15, 2014 - Fund Raising for Quarter 2, 2014 (MCID-103-14-15)
updates.body:

updates.body:
Nimal has been paralyzed waist down for the past 18 years, as a result of a wrong injection administered to her in childhood. She has gone through a lot of hospitalizations and medical procedures; and her family is still struggling to fund her treatment. MicroCharity has been supporting her with Rs.10,000 every quarter or Rs. 40,000 every year. The family has always been extremely grateful for our support, and has never asked for more. But MicroCharity team felt that it would be good to increase the amount considering their increased expenses, to Rs.15,000 a quarter. Requesting all donors to please stand with us in this decision to increase our donation amount.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 15,000.

June 10,2020 – Fund raising closed for this cause. We have disbursed the amount to Nimal against her medical bills.

MicroCharity is honored to continue supporting Nimal, who has been bedridden for the past 18 years. Listen to her story here, https://youtu.be/ZxetuOF6QPM (Malayalam video)

Joyner stays with his father, mother, elder brother, and younger sister. His father is the only earning member of the family, whose meager income is just enough to sustain the family. Joyner’s college fee for a year is INR 99,980 our of which they need help with the tuition fee which comes to INR 27,980.

title: 8th grade fees for Priyanka
summary: Priyanka is studying in 8th standard in a school in Bangalore, and doing well in her studies. Her family has no means to pay Priyanka's school fee, and she approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: Mar 10, 2021 - Pathanamthitta, Kerala (MCID-121-20-21)
updates.body:
Priyanka’s dad Peter, whom MicroCharity had supported in his surgery[Link here: https://microcharity.com/web/medical-expenses-for-a-sick-man/] was working as a painter, and her mother, Mary Sheela was working as a housemaid. But during Covid, both of them lost their jobs.

Her sister, Maria Monica is doing her second year Engineering course. Priyanka is studying in 8th standard in a school in Bangalore, and doing well in her studies.

The family has no means to pay Priyanka’s school fee, and she approached MicroCharity for financial assistance. Her total fee for this year comes to Rs. 50,000, out of which they have managed to pay Rs. 10,000. We are glad to help this smart little girl study well and achieve her dreams.

title: Shoulder surgery for Rahul
summary: 33-year old Rahul, the only earning member of his family has Obstetric brachial plexus injury from birth. He has been managing with constant physiotherapy and medication for the past many years and struggling with shoulder pain. He has to undergo multiple surgeries to cure this condition.

updates.caption: Mar 20, 2021 - Tripunithura, Kerala (MCID-122-20-21)
updates.body:
33-year old Rahul, the only earning member of his family has Obstetric brachial plexus injury from birth. He has been managing with constant physiotherapy and medication for the past many years and struggling with shoulder pain. He has been working as a marketing professional in a small organization, earning about INR 20,000 per month. His father is a heart patient who is no longer able to work. His mother is a housewife, and his sister is also not employed.

Recently Rahul met a doctor at Amrita hospital who confirmed that his shoulder issue can be cured with surgery. Both right and left shoulders need surgery, which would together cost INR 3-4 lakhs. Rahul underwent the first surgery on March 19, 2021 and is currently in the ICU. His friends and well-wishers are trying multiple means to collect funds before he is discharged, but they have met little success so far. The family approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: Mar 25, 2021 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request for INR 1,00,000.

updates.caption: Apr 05, 2021 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to the hospital.

title: Leg surgery for Anoop Kumar
summary: Anoop has undergone 9 surgeries already, he has a couple of surgeries more to go, which must be done on time to enable proper growth of the leg bones. Doctors are taking parts of the bone from his hip and grafting it into the leg, allowing the bones to grow naturally. He badly needs our support to get back on his feet.

updates.caption: Apr 1, 2021 - Edappally, Kerala (MCID-100-21-22)
updates.body:
Anoop came back from his work in the Kuwait Ministry of Defense in June 2019, to his house in Edapally, Kerala, to take care of his father who was affected with liver cirrhosis. But in February of 2020, he met with an accident when a speeding KSRTC bus hit his vehicle. His knee bone was broken completely, and a bone in the shin, Tibia, was also crushed completely.

Anoop has undergone 9 surgeries already, he has a couple of surgeries more to go, which must be done on time to enable proper growth of the leg bones. Doctors are taking parts of the bone from his hip and grafting it into the leg, allowing the bones to grow naturally.

His father was running a workshop, where Anoop was helping out after returning from Kuwait. But due to Covid, the workshop has not been getting enough work, reducing employees from 25 to 2 in a short span of time. Anoop’s next surgery is in 7 days, and he needs Rs. 1.5 lakhs for it. The family has spent all they could to fund his previous surgeries and are in no position to support this one. They are desperately in need of financial assistance and they approached MicroCharity for support.

updates.caption: Apr 7, 2021 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request for INR 75,000.

updates.caption: Apr 28, 2021 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. Amount Rs 75,000 is transferred to Hospital and surgery is successfully completed.

title: School fee for tribal children in Assam
summary: DeMatha English Medium School is a school in the tribal area of Kamrup district in Assam, run by the Trinitarians, priests belonging to The Order of the Most Holy Trinity. Most of the students are from the surrounding villages, which are economically backward areas. The villagers earn their income from cattle rearing and daily wage work, and are not keen on sending their children to school.

updates.caption: Apr 10, 2021 - Kamrup, Assam (MCID-101-21-22)
updates.body:
DeMatha English Medium School is a school in the tribal area of Kamrup district in Assam, run by the Trinitarians, priests belonging to The Order of the Most Holy Trinity. Most of the students are from the surrounding villages, which are economically backward areas. The villagers earn their income from cattle rearing and daily wage work, and are not keen on sending their children to school.

The Trinitarian fathers, as part of their mission, are encouraging the children to come, even if their parents cannot afford the school fee. Total expenses for one child per year comes to Rs. 10,000 including admission fee, monthly fee, books, and uniform. But there are many parents who cannot afford this amount. 

So the priests reached out to MicroCharity for financial assistance in sponsoring 5 children. For a meager sum of Rs.10,000, we can lift the lives of children who would otherwise spend their lives illiterate, without any job opportunities. We appeal to our donors to help us in this cause, so that the children can start attending school.

updates.caption: Apr 22, 2021 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 50,000 to sponsor 5 children.

updates.caption: Apr 30, 2021 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to the school.

title: House construction for Ashokan
summary: Ashokan was working as a daily wage laborer when he suffered from kidney failure, three years back. He is currently undergoing regular dialysis and is not able to work. His wife has some uterine infections, and doctors have recommended Hysterectomy to remove the uterus. But the family does not have enough money to pay for this surgery, and it is currently postponed.

updates.caption: Apr 21, 2021 - Nalukody, Changanassery, Kerala (MCID-102-21-22)
updates.body:
Ashokan was working as a daily wage laborer when he suffered from kidney failure, three years back. He is currently undergoing regular dialysis and is not able to work. His wife has some uterine infections, and doctors have recommended Hysterectomy to remove the uterus. But the family does not have enough money to pay for this surgery, and it is currently postponed.

They are staying with their four children – three daughters and a son, and also Ashokan’s mother in law, in a rented house at Rs. 4500 per month. The family has no source of income, and no means to pay for the children’s education, house rent and their daily household expenses.

JCI Nalukody has donated some land for the family to build a house, and is also planning the house construction soon. JCI in association with MicroCharity is supporting this poor family have a roof over their heads.

updates.caption: Apr 28, 2021 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs.8 lakh for the house construction.

title: Dialysis for Sinomon
summary: Sinomon from Idukki, Kerala has been suffering from kidney failure for the past 5 years and is undergoing regular dialysis. Each dialysis costs about INR 1,000 and he need about 2 or 3 sessions a week. Kidney transplant is the only solution, but the family has no means to go ahead with it. 3 months back, while going for dialysis, Sinomon met with an accident and suffered a leg fracture. So currently he needs treatment for that as well.

updates.caption: May 1, 2021 - Idukki, Kerala (MCID-103-21-22)
updates.body:
Sinomon from Idukki, Kerala has been suffering from kidney failure for the past 5 years and is undergoing regular dialysis. Each dialysis costs about INR 1,000 and he need about 2 or 3 sessions a week. Kidney transplant is the only solution, but the family has no means to go ahead with it. 3 months back, while going for dialysis, Sinomon met with an accident and suffered a leg fracture. So currently he needs treatment for that as well.

He was working as a daily wage laborer, but currently he is not able to work after the leg injury. Currently, he is currently staying in a home for the destitute where they provide at least the basic meals. Sinomon’s mother goes for some government-sponsored daily wage work, which is the only income for the family. But that is not regular, and she hardly earns enough to sustain the family. They reached out to MicroCharity for help with the dialysis.

updates.caption: May 6, 2021 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 50,000.

updates.caption: May 18, 2021 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Sinomon’s Hospital.

title: House construction for Ouseph Xavier
summary: Ouseph from Alapuzha, Kerala has been struggling with his house construction, fate dealing him not just one, but two blows affecting his life and his family. He was working as a fisherman, and had started constructing his house when he suffered a stroke. After some medication and rest, he recovered enough to restart the construction. But a second stroke soon after, left him paralyzed on one side of the body. Now he is not able to work or move around by himself.

updates.caption: May 15, 2021 - Alapuzha, Kerala (MCID-104-21-22)
updates.body:
Ouseph from Alapuzha, Kerala has been struggling with his house construction, fate dealing him not just one, but two blows affecting his life and his family. He was working as a fisherman, and had started constructing his house when he suffered a stroke. After some medication and rest, he recovered enough to restart the construction. But a second stroke soon after, left him paralyzed on one side of the body. Now he is not able to work or move around by himself.

His wife, Rosamma is employed through a government project, but there is no regular job. She earns Rs. 3500 per month and is the only earning member of the family. Their son Alan is doing his Plus two, studying in a seminary, and daughter Alayana is in 8th standard.

The family lives in the coastal area of Alapuzha in a temporary shed made of wooden planks, covered with tarpaulin. They’ve been struggling in this single room structure for the past 4 years, unable to complete the house. Now, the priests at Holy Trinity Ashram have promised to help with doors and windows for the house. They need Rs. 87,000 more to complete the house, and they approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: May 20, 2021 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 87,000.

updates.caption: May 29, 2021 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. We have raised Rs 87,000 from 5 micro donations in 9 days. Construction will be starting soon.

title: Medical Support for Nimal – June 2021
summary: Nimal and family still needs our financial assistance, since she has been bedridden for many years now. MicroCharity continues to support her every quarter with our prayers and contributions.

updates.caption: Oct 1, 2012 - Kochuthovala, Kattappana, Kerala (MCID-111-12)
updates.body: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 10 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. The family is unable to repay the loans as they have to spend on the treatment. She had a tumour in uterus and she had to go through a hysterectomy. Now she is also suffering from kidney stone problems.

updates.caption: Oct 16, 2012 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body:
Microcharity is supporting Nimal and her family by bearing a part of the medical expenses. Microcharity approved Rs. 10,000 for the year 2012. The plan is to raise Rs 10,000 for her in each quarter of the year and continuously support her in medical expenses.

Oct 18, 2012: MicroCharity raised Rs. 10,000 from 8 different individuals in 2 days. The fund is transferred to Nimal’s family.

updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well these days. She is now admitted at a hospital due to continuous nausea and vomiting. This is due to the hormone level issues she has been fighting with. The fund raised by MicroCharity was utilized to treat the hormone issue. She is also suffering from severe muscle strain. MicroCharity decided to continue supporting her by raising Rs. 10,000 for the first quarter of 2013.

Feb 4, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 6 different donors in 6 days.

March 10,2020 – Fund raising closed for this cause. We will be paying he amount to Nimal against her medical bills.

updates.caption: June 01, 2020 - Fund Raising for Quarter 3, 2020 (MCID-100-20-21)
updates.body:
Nimal has been paralyzed waist down for the past 18 years, as a result of a wrong injection administered to her in childhood. She has gone through a lot of hospitalizations and medical procedures; and her family is still struggling to fund her treatment. MicroCharity has been supporting her with Rs.10,000 every quarter or Rs. 40,000 every year. The family has always been extremely grateful for our support, and has never asked for more. But MicroCharity team felt that it would be good to increase the amount considering their increased expenses, to Rs.15,000 a quarter. Requesting all donors to please stand with us in this decision to increase our donation amount.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 15,000.

June 10,2020 – Fund raising closed for this cause. We have disbursed the amount to Nimal against her medical bills.

MicroCharity is honored to continue supporting Nimal, who has been bedridden for the past 18 years. Listen to her story here, https://youtu.be/ZxetuOF6QPM (Malayalam video)

title: Medical Support for Babukuttan
summary: Babukuttan from Idukki, Kerala was diagnosed with cancer a few months back. To make things worse he was infected with corona virus during the hospitalization. As of now he is on ventilator fighting with post covid complications.

updates.caption: Jun 10, 2021 - Idukki, Kerala (MCID-106-21-22)
updates.body:
Babukuttan from Idukki, Kerala was diagnosed with cancer a few months back. To make things worse he was infected with corona virus during the hospitalization. As of now he is on ventilator fighting with post covid complications.

Babukuttan lives in Vellakkayam, Idukki with his wife and two school going children and works as a news paper agent. He is currently admitted at Carithas Hospital, Kottayam for over a month and is on ventilator now with a critical condition. He is the only breadwinner in the family and the family is going through a financial struggle. Babukuttan’s family badly needs our support to meet the medical expenses.

updates.caption: Jun 15, 2021 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 1,00,000.

updates.caption: Jun 23, 2021 - Update
updates.body: Babukuttan was discharged from Carithas Hospital on Jun 21 and was shifted to Medical College, Idukki. He left this world to receive his eternal rest in the early hours of the following day.

updates.caption: Jui 1, 2021 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. INR 75000 was paid to Carithas Hospital as he was discharged. The remaining amount will be paid to his family.

title: Medical Support for Thasmiya Banu
summary: 20 year old Thasmiya Banu was in the second month of pregnancy when she was paralysed by a rare disorder called Guillain Barre Syndrome. She gave birth to a preterm baby and hopefully they both survived the ordeal.

updates.caption: Jul 1, 2021 - Hindupur, Andhra Pradesh (MCID-107-21-22)
updates.body:
20 year old Thasmiya Banu was in the second month of pregnancy when she was paralysed by a rare disorder called Guillain Barre Syndrome. She gave birth to a preterm baby and hopefully they both survived the ordeal. 

Thasmiya is still under treatment and now on a wheel chair. Her father who works as a welder and her two younger siblings are struggling to meet the expenses of the treatment and they need our help.

updates.caption: Jul 6, 2021 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 50,000.

updates.caption: Ju1 30, 2021 - Fund Raising Closed

title: Nursing school fee for Anjitha
summary: Anjitha from Mundakkayam, Kerala is doing her 1st year BSc Nursing course in a Nursing school in Bangalore. Her father is a daily wage laborer and mother works as a contract employee, neither of them having regular work. Her sister Anjaly is a student. The family has barely enough income to sustain their daily expenses.

updates.caption: Jul 10, 2021 - Mundakkayam, Kerala (MCID-108-21-22)
updates.body:
Anjitha from Mundakkayam, Kerala is doing her 1st year BSc Nursing course in a Nursing school in Bangalore. Her father is a daily wage laborer and mother works as a contract employee, neither of them having regular work. Her sister Anjaly is a student. The family has barely enough income to sustain their daily expenses.

Anjitha joined college paying Rs.35,000, the bare minimum needed for admissions. Her 1st year exam is in August, and she needs to pay the full fee of Rs. 1.5 lakhs to attend the examinations.

The family is struggling to raise money during this time of the pandemic, when jobs are difficult to come by. They approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: Aug 06, 2021 - Fund Raising Closed

title: House construction for Maniyan
summary: Maniyan and family need our help in completing their house construction, which currently only has a single room on a foundation. This was built with Rs. 2 lakhs received from the government. Maniyan is disabled, and is not able to work. He lives with his family consisting of his wife and 3 children; 2 of them still in school.

updates.caption: Aug 1, 2021 - Erumeli, Kerala (MCID-112-21-22)
updates.body:
Maniyan and family need our help in completing their house construction, which currently only has a single room on a foundation. This was built with Rs. 2 lakhs received from the government. Maniyan is disabled, and is not able to work. He lives with his family consisting of his wife and 3 children; 2 of them still in school.

His wife is working as a housemaid, and the family’s monthly income is only Rs. 10,000, which is barely enough to meet their daily needs and children’s education. They are desperate to build a bathroom attached to the house, so that the girls will not have to go outside into the makeshift bathroom they have put together outside the house.

A bathroom and small kitchen need to be added to the existing house, which will cost Rs. 3 lakhs. The family does not have any money to spare for this, and they approached MicroCharity for financial support.

updates.caption: Aug 5, 2021 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 75,000.

updates.caption: Aug 30, 2021 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. Construction will start soon!

title: Nursing College Fee for Shalat
summary: Shalat, daughter of Shaji and Shanty from Pathanamthitta, Kerala will have to discontinue her Nursing course without our financial support. Her father Shaji is a farmer who is not able to work these days since he has a steel rod inserted in his legs post a recent accident. Her mother is a home maker and younger sister is still in school. The family is struggling to meet their daily expenses. Microcharity is supporting her with fee for last 3 years and now she is 3rd year. She need all our support

updates.caption: April 3, 2018 - Pathanamthitta, Kerala (MCID-103-18-19)
updates.body:
Shalat, daughter of Shaji and Shanty from Pathanamthitta, Kerala will have to discontinue her Nursing course without our financial support. Her father Shaji is a farmer who is not able to work these days since he has a steel rod inserted in his legs post a recent accident. Her mother is a home maker and younger sister is still in school. The family is struggling to meet their daily expenses.

Shalat got admissions in a private college where the total fee for first year is 97,500. They have already paid 31,000 and need our help in paying the rest. They approached MicroCharity for financial support.

updates.caption: April 23, 2018 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 55, 000.

updates.caption: May 04, 2018 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. We have raised Rs. 55, 000 from 16 micro donations in 11 days. We will be paying fee to college shortly

updates.caption: 2nd Year Fee, March 6, 2019 - Pathanamthitta, Kerala (MCID-131-18-19)
updates.body:
Shalat’s dad is now working as a security guard on night duty. The family is in no position to support her second year Nursing course. Shalat was stuggling with studies during the initial months since she had studied in a Malayalam-medium school, and the language switch was tough. But she is coping well now, and very keen to continue her studies.

The total fee including accomodation comes to Rs. 97,500, out of which the family has agreed to raise Rs.17,500.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 80,000.

updates.caption: May 13, 2019 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. We have raised Rs. 80, 000 from 19 micro donations in 7 days. Fee is directly transferred to college.

updates.caption: 3rd Year Fee, Feb 11, 2020 - Pathanamthitta, Kerala (MCID-120-19-20)
updates.body:
Shalat has completed her 2nd year BSc Nursing course and moved on to the 3rd year. The family is incapable of supporting her education, and they require our financial assistance this year as well. Total fee for 3rd year is Rs. 97,500.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 77,500

updates.caption: 4th Year Fee, Aug 10, 2021 - Pathanamthitta, Kerala (MCID-113-21-22)
updates.body:
Shalat has now moved on to her final year of the BSc Nursing course. Her dad is not earning enough to support her education, and they are in need of our financial assistance.

Total fee for 4th year BSc Nursing is Rs. 97,500. The college had asked her not to attend classes since she has not paid the fee. With our request, she was granted one month’s time to clear the dues. So this is an urgent cause, and we need your active support to help Shalat.

MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 75,000

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. We have raised Rs. 75,000 and  Fee is  directly transferred to college.

title: JCI India : Project KAVAJ (Protection) – Donate Vaccine, to save Life!
summary: The State of Kerala, is at the threshold of a third wave of the current pandemic situation. A big challenge to the Government and for the public in general is to get Covid vaccination to 3.50 Crores citizen's.

updates.caption: Sep 01, 2021 - JCI India Zone XXII, Kerala (MCID-114-21-22)
updates.body:
The State of Kerala, is at the threshold of a third wave of the current pandemic situation. A big challenge to the Government and for the public in general is to get Covid vaccination to 3.50 Crores citizen’s.

JCI which believes in “Service to Humanity is the best work of Life” is in the forefront of this big challenge by supporting the government in its initiative to make Covid Free Kerala.

JCI India Zone XXII has tied up with many private hospitals to provide free vaccination to the marginalized communities and support to the technology illiterate, palliative care, help needed categories in the society by providing easy access to Covid vaccine.

The KAVAJ (Protection) project is initiated by JCI India Zone XXII which covers districts of Trivandrum, Kollam, Pathanamthitta, Kottayam,Alappuzha and Idukki. The project will be executed by private hospitals supported by respective JCI Local Organisations present at different locations.

We seek out like minded individuals, institutions and corporates who can provide funds for free vaccination. This endeavor ensures channelizing funds for vaccinating the deserving candidates who cannot afford to pay for the dose.

All donations are eligible for Tax exemption U/s 80G of Income Tax Act.

Let’s join together to fight against the pandemic for a Covid Free ’God’s Own Country’ !

updates.caption: Sep 01, 2021 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body:
MicroCharity approves Project Kavach fund raising where public can donate in multiple of each vaccine cost.

Cost of Single vaccine is Rs 780 which will be the minimum donation amount and one can donate any amount which is multiples of Rs. 780

title: Medical Support for Nimal – September 2021
summary: Nimal and family still needs our financial assistance, since she has been bedridden for many years now. MicroCharity continues to support her every quarter with our prayers and contributions.

updates.caption: Oct 1, 2012 - Kochuthovala, Kattappana, Kerala (MCID-111-12)
updates.body: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 10 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. The family is unable to repay the loans as they have to spend on the treatment. She had a tumour in uterus and she had to go through a hysterectomy. Now she is also suffering from kidney stone problems.

updates.caption: Oct 16, 2012 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body:
Microcharity is supporting Nimal and her family by bearing a part of the medical expenses. Microcharity approved Rs. 10,000 for the year 2012. The plan is to raise Rs 10,000 for her in each quarter of the year and continuously support her in medical expenses.

Oct 18, 2012: MicroCharity raised Rs. 10,000 from 8 different individuals in 2 days. The fund is transferred to Nimal’s family.

updates.caption: January 29, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 1, 2013
updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well these days. She is now admitted at a hospital due to continuous nausea and vomiting. This is due to the hormone level issues she has been fighting with. The fund raised by MicroCharity was utilized to treat the hormone issue. She is also suffering from severe muscle strain. MicroCharity decided to continue supporting her by raising Rs. 10,000 for the first quarter of 2013.

Feb 4, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 6 different donors in 6 days.

Joyner stays with his father, mother, elder brother, and younger sister. His father is the only earning member of the family, whose meager income is just enough to sustain the family. Joyner’s college fee for a year is INR 99,980 out of which they need help with the tuition fee which comes to INR 27,980.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 28,000, which would be the tuition fee for the second year.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Joyner’s college.

title: Chemotherapy Treatment for Manjusha Binu
summary: Manjusha and her family are struggling with medical expenses for her breast cancer treatment, when they were already burdened with the expenses of a surgery to help their 5-year-old-son walk again. Manjusha was diagnosed with cancer a few months back and went through surgery to remove one of her breasts.

updates.caption: Oct 10, 2021 - Aluva, Kerala (MCID-117-21-22)
updates.body:
Manjusha and her family are struggling with medical expenses for her breast cancer treatment, when they were already burdened with the expenses of a surgery to help their 5-year-old-son walk again. Manjusha was diagnosed with cancer a few months back and went through surgery to remove one of her breasts.

She is staying with her husband who is a daily wage labourer, and 3 children aged 15 years, 9 years, and 5 years. The youngest son, Alwin is suffering from cerebral palsy, and the family was staying in a rented house near the little boy’s school, for ease of transportation. He recently underwent a surgery to help him walk.

Manjusha’s doctor has advised chemotherapy and radiation, the total cost of which would come to INR 1 lakh. The family has no way to pay for this treatment, since they are struggling with their daily expenses. They approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: MicroCharity approves fund request!
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 25,000.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Manjusha’s Hospital.

title: Nursing School Fee for Tincy

Her father is working as a coolie at Rs. 5000 per month, and does not have regular work. Her mother is not working, and her younger brother is still in school.

Her father is working as a coolie at Rs. 5000 per month, and does not have regular work. Her mother is not working, and her younger brother is still in school.

The family stays in a small house with an asbestos roof. Tincy’s father is not in a position to support the family’s daily expenses and both the children’s education. Tincy is good at studies and wants to complete her education, so that she can take care of her family.

She got admissions at a Nursing college in Thalassery, Kerala. The total fee for this academic year comes to Rs. 76,000 and the family has no way to fund this. So they approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: MicroCharity approves fund request!

title: Post-kidney transplant treatment for Lijo Thomas
summary: Lijo needs our help in his post-kidney transplant care and treatment. He has been suffering from Polycystic Kidney Disease (PKD) for the past 12 years, undergoing regular dialysis. One of his kidneys was removed in 2018. In September 2021, he underwent a kidney transplant under the Kerala government’s Mrithasanjeevani scheme.

updates.caption: Dec 1, 2021 - Muvattupuzha, Kerala (MCID-117-17-18)
updates.body:
Lijo needs our help in his post-kidney transplant care and treatment. He has been suffering from Polycystic Kidney Disease (PKD) for the past 12 years, undergoing regular dialysis. One of his kidneys was removed in 2018. In September 2021, he underwent a kidney transplant under the Kerala government’s Mrithasanjeevani scheme.

After the transplant, he needs to stay near the hospital for 3 months, so that the doctor can visit for regular checkups. He also needs medicines worth Rs. 35,000 for the next 4-6 months. Lijo was working as an electrician doing small-scale repair jobs since his health did not allow him to do any heavy jobs.

Lijo lost his mother to cancer in 2000 and his father died in 2010 after prolonged treatment for his kidney disease. He had to spend a lot of money for the treatment of both his parents, and this has left him in debt. The family has no means to support Lijo’s post-operative treatment, with his wife’s daily wage jobs. They are in desperate need of financial aid and reached out to MicroCharity for assistance.

updates.caption: Dec 7, 2021 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 50,000.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Lijo’s Hospital.

title: Medical Support for Nimal – December 2021
summary: Nimal and family still needs our financial assistance, since she has been bedridden for many years now. MicroCharity continues to support her every quarter with our prayers and contributions.

updates.caption: Oct 1, 2012 - Kochuthovala, Kattappana, Kerala (MCID-111-12)
updates.body: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 10 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. The family is unable to repay the loans as they have to spend on the treatment. She had a tumour in uterus and she had to go through a hysterectomy. Now she is also suffering from kidney stone problems.

updates.caption: Oct 16, 2012 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body:
Microcharity is supporting Nimal and her family by bearing a part of the medical expenses. Microcharity approved Rs. 10,000 for the year 2012. The plan is to raise Rs 10,000 for her in each quarter of the year and continuously support her in medical expenses.

Oct 18, 2012: MicroCharity raised Rs. 10,000 from 8 different individuals in 2 days. The fund is transferred to Nimal’s family.

updates.caption: January 29, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 1, 2013
updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well these days. She is now admitted at a hospital due to continuous nausea and vomiting. This is due to the hormone level issues she has been fighting with. The fund raised by MicroCharity was utilized to treat the hormone issue. She is also suffering from severe muscle strain. MicroCharity decided to continue supporting her by raising Rs. 10,000 for the first quarter of 2013.

Feb 4, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 6 different donors in 6 days.

updates.caption: April 30, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 2, 2013
updates.body:

updates.caption: Aug 17, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 3, 2013
updates.body:

Aug 21, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 10 different micro donations in 4 days.

The family is unable to keep up with all the medical expenses. MicroCharity approves an increased quarterly support amount of Rs. 50,000 this time, to help the family with the medical expenses.

title: 9th grade fees for Priyanka
summary: Priyanka is studying in 8th standard in a school in Bangalore, and doing well in her studies. Her family has no means to pay Priyanka's school fee, and she approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: Mar 10, 2021 - Pathanamthitta, Kerala (MCID-121-20-21)
updates.body:
Priyanka’s dad Peter, whom MicroCharity had supported in his surgery[Link here] was working as a painter, and her mother, Mary Sheela was working as a housemaid. But during Covid, both of them lost their jobs.

Her sister, Maria Monica is doing her second year Engineering course. Priyanka is studying in 8th standard in a school in Bangalore, and doing well in her studies.

The family has no means to pay Priyanka’s school fee, and she approached MicroCharity for financial assistance. Her total fee for this year comes to Rs. 50,000, out of which they have managed to pay Rs. 10,000. We are glad to help this smart little girl study well and achieve her dreams.

updates.caption: Mar 15, 2021 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request for INR 27,500.

updates.caption: Jan 10, 2022 - Fund Raising for 9th Grade (MCID-123-21-22)
updates.body:
Priyanka’s dad is in no position to work due to his health condition. Her mother tried running a catering business, but did not receive enough orders to earn money to sustain her family. They have no other source of income, and is struggling to pay Priyanka’s school fee.

They reached out to MicroCharity to help pay Priyanka’s school fee.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 25,000.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Priyanka’s college.

title: 10th standard fee for Aleena Tomy
summary: Aleena's father, Tomy was running a restaurant in Chennai, which closed down recently due to Covid. During the same time, his mother-in-law was diagnosed with cancer, and the family had to spend a lot of money on her treatment.

updates.caption: Jan 10, 2022 - Chennai, Tamil Nadu(MCID-123-21-22)
updates.body:
Aleena’s father, Tomy was running a restaurant in Chennai, which closed down recently due to Covid. During the same time, his mother-in-law was diagnosed with cancer, and the family had to spend a lot of money on her treatment.

They are now struggling to pay the 10th standard fee for Aleena, which comes to INR 34,000. Since they don’t have any other source of income, the family approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: Jan 20, 2022 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request for INR 25,000.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Aleena’s school.

title: SMA Treatment for a young child
summary: Guruchith, an 8 year old boy is suffering from a very rare degenerative progressive life threatening neuromuscular disease - Spinal Muscular Atrophy (SMA). Guruchith is son of Ajikesh P, a member of Vijayee Bhava Batch 9(VB9).

updates.caption: Feb 1, 2022 - Kottayam, Kerala (MCID-124-21-22)
updates.body:
Guruchith, an 8 year old boy is suffering from a very rare degenerative progressive life threatening neuromuscular disease – Spinal Muscular Atrophy (SMA). Guruchith is son of Ajikesh P, a member of Vijayee Bhava Batch 9(VB9). Unfortunately, the only available treatment is a very expensive drug called ’Risdiplam’. The cost of the treatment is way too high for the family to bear.

 

As fellow VBians, it is our responsibility to support Ajikesh in this difficult time. His batch mates from VB9 are determined to raise at least 5 lakh rupees to support Ajikesh in his fight for his child’s life. Let’s hope and pray that Guruchith will be able to defeat this dreadful progressive illness.

 

Inviting every VBian to be a part of this noble cause! Vijayee Bhava!

The fund raising is organized through MicroCharity, a zero profit charity organization, who doesn’t charge any fees on fund raising.

title: Nursing College Fee for Ann Merry Ruth
summary: Ann Merry needs our help in starting her BSc Nursing course at a college in Coimbatore. Her father, Robert is an auto-driver who hires an auto on daily rent, earning about Rs. 6,000 per month. Her mother was working as an assistant at a hospital. But she had to leave that job to look after her bed-ridden mother and father-in-law.

updates.caption: Feb 20, 2022 - Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu (MCID-125-21-22)
updates.body:
Ann Merry needs our help in starting her BSc Nursing course at a college in Coimbatore. Her father, Robert is an auto-driver who hires an auto on daily rent, earning about Rs. 6,000 per month. Her mother was working as an assistant at a hospital. But she had to leave that job to look after her bed-ridden mother and father-in-law.

Robert is living in their ancestral house, which is part-owned by his brothers and sisters. He approached many banks for an education loan for Ann Merry’s studies, but he was refused since he does not have any property in his name.

The family also has other loans to pay off, and they are in no position to fund Ann Merry’s education. They approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Ann Merry’s college.

title: Medical Support for Nimal – March 2022
summary: Nimal and family still needs our financial assistance, since she has been bedridden for many years now. MicroCharity continues to support her every quarter with our prayers and contributions.

updates.caption: Oct 1, 2012 - Kochuthovala, Kattappana, Kerala (MCID-111-12)
updates.body: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 10 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. The family is unable to repay the loans as they have to spend on the treatment. She had a tumour in uterus and she had to go through a hysterectomy. Now she is also suffering from kidney stone problems.

updates.caption: Oct 16, 2012 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body:
Microcharity is supporting Nimal and her family by bearing a part of the medical expenses. Microcharity approved Rs. 10,000 for the year 2012. The plan is to raise Rs 10,000 for her in each quarter of the year and continuously support her in medical expenses.

Oct 18, 2012: MicroCharity raised Rs. 10,000 from 8 different individuals in 2 days. The fund is transferred to Nimal’s family.

updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well these days. She is now admitted at a hospital due to continuous nausea and vomiting. This is due to the hormone level issues she has been fighting with. The fund raised by MicroCharity was utilized to treat the hormone issue. She is also suffering from severe muscle strain. MicroCharity decided to continue supporting her by raising Rs. 10,000 for the first quarter of 2013.

title: Cancer Treatment for Devadasan
summary: 48 year old Devadasan and family are struggling to fund his cancer treatment – surgery and post-operative expenses. He had a surgery for ulcer about 5 years back and was working as a welder at a very small income when tragedy struck. 6 months back, he fell ill and was taken to the hospital. Doctors diagnosed cancer of the stomach and intestines. They recommended a jejunotomy surgery, which was completed immediately at P K DAS Institute of Medical Sciences, Vaniamkulam.

updates.caption: Mar 5, 2022 - Palakkad, Kerala (MCID-127-21-22)
updates.body:
48 year old Devadasan and family are struggling to fund his cancer treatment – surgery and post-operative expenses. He had a surgery for ulcer about 5 years back and was working as a welder at a very small income when tragedy struck. 6 months back, he fell ill and was taken to the hospital. Doctors diagnosed cancer of the stomach and intestines. They recommended a jejunotomy surgery, which was completed immediately at P K DAS Institute of Medical Sciences, Vaniamkulam.

Devadasan has been in the hospital for 20 days following surgery and is currently too weak, for them to start chemotherapy. Doctors are waiting for his health to improve before deciding on further treatment. Total expenses have already crossed Rs. 1.35 lakhs, Rs. 1.1 lakhs for the treatment and Rs. 25,000 for medicines.

His wife does small jobs as part of the Kerala government’s ‘thozilurappu’ scheme, earning very little each month. Devadasan was also taking care of his bed-ridden and paralyzed 46-year-old younger brother, and also his sister who stays in the same house to take care of the younger brother. The family has more burdens than they can carry, and with Devadasan falling ill, they have no other source of income.

They are struggling to meet his medical expenses, and reached out to MicroCharity for financial assistance.

MicroCharity approves financial support of Rs. 50,000

updates.caption: Fund raising closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this case. Fund will be transferred directly to the hospital.

title: Artificial leg for John Britto
summary: John had been suffering from varicose veins on his legs for the past 2 years. In May 2021, there was a serious infection, post which the doctors had to amputate 70 cm of his left leg. He used to sell fish from his two-wheeler before the surgery, but is unable to work now.

updates.caption: Mar 10, 2022 - Kollam, Kerala (MCID-130-21-22)
updates.body:
John had been suffering from varicose veins on his legs for the past 2 years. In May 2021, there was a serious infection, post which the doctors had to amputate 70 cm of his left leg. He used to sell fish from his two-wheeler before the surgery, but is unable to work now.

His wife was retired as an employee at a cashew factory, and is earning a monthly pension of Rs. 2500. John is very keen on getting a prosthetic leg fitted, so that he can earn a living and support his family. They enquired at an Artificial Limb Center and came to know that prosthetic legs cost between Rs. 60,000 to 12 lakhs.

The family has very little regular income, and is in no position to buy this artificial leg for John. So they approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: Mar 20, 2022 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 75,000.

updates.caption: Mar 31, 2022 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to John’s hospital.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 50,000

updates.caption: Fundraising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause.

title: Kidney Transplant for Sinomon
summary: Sinomon had been undergoing 3 dialysis sessions per week for past few years. Doctors have recommended kidney transplantation as the only solution. They have found a donor, and the transplantation surgery is scheduled for May 17th, 2022. The total cost for surgery and post-operative treatment is approximately Rs. 12 lakhs. Sinomon’s family is in dire need of financial assistance, since there are no other earning members.

updates.caption: May 1, 2021 - Idukki, Kerala (MCID-103-21-22)
updates.body:
Sinomon from Idukki, Kerala has been suffering from kidney failure for the past 5 years and is undergoing regular dialysis. Each dialysis costs about INR 1,000 and he need about 2 or 3 sessions a week. Kidney transplant is the only solution, but the family has no means to go ahead with it. 3 months back, while going for dialysis, Sinomon met with an accident and suffered a leg fracture. So currently he needs treatment for that as well.

He was working as a daily wage laborer, but currently he is not able to work after the leg injury. Currently, he is currently staying in a home for the destitute where they provide at least the basic meals. Sinomon’s mother goes for some government-sponsored daily wage work, which is the only income for the family. But that is not regular, and she hardly earns enough to sustain the family. They reached out to MicroCharity for help with the dialysis.

updates.caption: May 6, 2021 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 50,000.

updates.caption: May 6, 2022 - Fund Raising for Kidney Transplant (MCID-103-22-23)
updates.body:
Sinomon had been undergoing 3 dialysis sessions per week for past few years. Doctors have recommended kidney transplantation as the only solution. They have found a donor, and the transplantation surgery is scheduled for May 17th, 2022.

The total cost for surgery and post-operative treatment is approximately Rs. 12 lakhs. Sinomon’s family is in dire need of financial assistance, since there are no other earning members. 

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 1 lakh.

updates.caption: May 25, 2022 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Sinomon’s Hospital.

title: School fee for Jeraldina and Jeromina
summary: Twin sisters, Jeraldina and Jeromina’s story is not different from many lower income families in Bangalore, India. Their dad Sesu Raj, the sole bread winner of the family makes about Rs.200 per day working as an auto driver. But he is a chronic alcoholic and does not go to work regularly. The family is now struggling to get the kids to school. The little girls are in 1st standard and at a risk of not going to school at all, due to their financial difficulties. They have other loans to pay off and also a monthly rent of Rs.1500 for the house they are staying in.

updates.caption: May 10, 2017 - Bangalore, Karnataka (MCID-105-17-18)
updates.body:
Twin sisters, Jeraldina and Jeromina’s story is not different from many lower income families in Bangalore, India. Their dad Sesu Raj, the sole bread winner of the family makes about Rs.200 per day working as an auto driver. But he is a chronic alcoholic and does not go to work regularly. The family is now struggling to get the kids to school. The little girls are in 1st standard and at a risk of not going to school at all, due to their financial difficulties. They have other loans to pay off and also a monthly rent of Rs.1500 for the house they are staying in.

The fee for both children combined come to Rs.25,700 for this year. The family approached MicroCharity for assistance in paying the school fee.

updates.caption: May 30, 2017 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs.21,000.

updates.caption: June 1, 2017 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. Rs.21,000 is raised from 6 micro donations in 2 days. We will paying the fee shortly to school.

updates.caption: May 14, 2018 - Bangalore, Karnataka (MCID-104-18-19)
updates.body:
Jeraldina and Jeromina are doing really well in their studies. Jeraldina scored above 90% for all subjects, and Jeromina above 70%. Both are going to third standard this academic year. The family is still struggling to pay the school fee, and approached MicroCharity for financial support.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 25,000.

updates.caption: May 19, 2018 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. Rs.25,000 is raised from 5 micro donations in 5 days. We will paying the fee shortly to school.

updates.caption: July 3, 2019 - Bangalore, Karnataka (MCID-105-19-20)
updates.body:
Jeraldina and Jeromina are 2 diligent little girls, who will have to drop out of school without our support. They scored A or A+ for all subjects in 3rd grade and has been promoted to 4th. Their mother is still struggling to pay their school fee and approached MicroCharity for financial support.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 30,000.

updates.caption: Jun 1, 2022 - School Fee for 2022 (MCID-104-22-23)
updates.body:
Jeraldina and Jeromina are doing well at school. They did not have proper classes for 2 years due to Covid. School has started again and the family is not able to support their yearly fee of Rs. 44,700. Their father is working as an auto driver earning Rs.4,000 per month. So they approached MicroCharity for financial assistance. 

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 35,000.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause. School fee of Rs. 35,000 will be paid direct to the school.

title: Medical Support for Nimal – June 2022
summary: Nimal and family still needs our financial assistance, since she has been bedridden for many years now. MicroCharity continues to support her every quarter with our prayers and contributions.

updates.caption: Oct 1, 2012 - Kochuthovala, Kattappana, Kerala (MCID-111-12)
updates.body: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 10 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. The family is unable to repay the loans as they have to spend on the treatment. She had a tumour in uterus and she had to go through a hysterectomy. Now she is also suffering from kidney stone problems.

updates.caption: Oct 16, 2012 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body:
Microcharity is supporting Nimal and her family by bearing a part of the medical expenses. Microcharity approved Rs. 10,000 for the year 2012. The plan is to raise Rs 10,000 for her in each quarter of the year and continuously support her in medical expenses.

Oct 18, 2012: MicroCharity raised Rs. 10,000 from 8 different individuals in 2 days. The fund is transferred to Nimal’s family.

updates.caption: January 29, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 1, 2013
updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well these days. She is now admitted at a hospital due to continuous nausea and vomiting. This is due to the hormone level issues she has been fighting with. The fund raised by MicroCharity was utilized to treat the hormone issue. She is also suffering from severe muscle strain. MicroCharity decided to continue supporting her by raising Rs. 10,000 for the first quarter of 2013.

updates.caption: Nov 13, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 4, 2013

MicroCharity has been supporting her with Rs.10,000 every quarter or Rs. 40,000 every year. The family has always been extremely grateful for our support, and has never asked for more. But MicroCharity team felt that it would be good to increase the amount considering their increased expenses, to Rs.15,000 a quarter. Requesting all donors to please stand with us in this decision to increase our donation amount.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 15,000.

MicroCharity is honored to continue supporting Nimal, who has been bedridden for the past 18 years. Listen to her story here, https://youtu.be/ZxetuOF6QPM (Malayalam video)

title: Skull Surgery for Thresha
summary: Thresha, a 20-year old degree student is going through a very difficult time. She had a brain surgery 7 months back, but now the doctors are prescribing a cranioplasty to repair a defect in the skull. The involves inserting a titanium mesh to form the contour of the skull after lifting the scalp. She is currently suffering from CVT cerebral venous thrombosis or clots in the brain, and also paralysis on the right hand as well as leg.

updates.caption: Jun 1, 2022 - Bangalore, Karnataka (MCID-107-22-23)
updates.body:
Thresha, a 20-year old degree student is going through a very difficult time. She had a brain surgery 7 months back, but now the doctors are prescribing a cranioplasty to repair a defect in the skull. The involves inserting a titanium mesh to form the contour of the skull after lifting the scalp. She is currently suffering from CVT cerebral venous thrombosis or clots in the brain, and also paralysis on the right hand as well as leg. 

Thresha’s father is running a small shop after he lost his job during the pandemic. Her mother also gave up her job as an accountant to take care of Thresha. The family has very little money to take care of their daily expenses, and are struggling to meet medical expenses for their daughter. Total cost for the surgery will come to Rs. 75,000 in addition to medicine, room, and nursing care. Her family is in a dire situation, and reached out to MicroCharity for help.

updates.caption: Jun 6, 2022 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 45,000.

updates.caption: Jun 20, 2022 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Thresha’s Hospital.

title: School fee for tribal children in Assam
summary: DeMatha English Medium School is a school in the tribal area of Kamrup district in Assam, run by the Trinitarians, priests belonging to The Order of the Most Holy Trinity. Most of the students are from the surrounding villages, which are economically backward areas. The villagers earn their income from cattle rearing and daily wage work, and are not keen on sending their children to school.

updates.caption: Apr 10, 2021 - Kamrup, Assam (MCID-101-21-22)
updates.body:
DeMatha English Medium School is a school in the tribal area of Kamrup district in Assam, run by the Trinitarians, priests belonging to The Order of the Most Holy Trinity. Most of the students are from the surrounding villages, which are economically backward areas. The villagers earn their income from cattle rearing and daily wage work, and are not keen on sending their children to school.

The Trinitarian fathers, as part of their mission, are encouraging the children to come, even if their parents cannot afford the school fee. Total expenses for one child per year comes to Rs. 10,000 including admission fee, monthly fee, books, and uniform. But there are many parents who cannot afford this amount. 

So the priests reached out to MicroCharity for financial assistance in sponsoring 5 children. For a meager sum of Rs.10,000, we can lift the lives of children who would otherwise spend their lives illiterate, without any job opportunities. We appeal to our donors to help us in this cause, so that the children can start attending school.

updates.caption: Apr 22, 2021 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 50,000 to sponsor 5 children.

updates.caption: Aug 10, 2022 - Fund Raising for 2022 (MCID-108-22-23)
updates.body:
Tribal children at DeMatha English Medium School have a high chance of dropping out due to financial constraints. Trinitarian priests are doing their best to provide high-quality education to children from these villages, that are already suffering from low rates of literacy, which leads to higher rates of unemployment, crime, and alcoholism.

MicroCharity is supporting 2 children this year at a cost of Rs. 12,000 per student for uniform, books, and school fee.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 24,000.

updates.caption: Aug 30, 2022 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to the school.

title: School fee for children at Sanjoe Bhavan
summary: Sanjoe Bhavan is a home for orphans and street children from the surrounding slums at J P Nagar, Bangalore, India. Sisters of the Destitute congregation are running Sanjoe Bhavan, taking care of these children - their accomodation, food, education, and other daily needs.

updates.caption: Sep 1, 2022 - Bangalore, Karnataka (MCID-109-22-23)
updates.body:
Sanjoe Bhavan is a home for orphans and street children from the surrounding slums at J P Nagar, Bangalore, India. Sisters of the Destitute congregation are running Sanjoe Bhavan, taking care of these children – their accomodation, food, education, and other daily needs. 

They are now struggling to pay school fees for these children, approximately Rs. 22,000-32,000 per child per year. They reached out to MicroCharity for financial assistance. We would request our donors and well-wishers to wholeheartedly support these children, who would otherwise be denied basic education, and a safe home to live in.

updates.caption: Sep 1, 2022 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 80,000 to sponsor 3 children.

updates.caption: Sep 20, 2022 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to the school.

A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. The family is unable to repay the loans as they have to spend on the treatment. She had a tumour in uterus and she had to go through a hysterectomy. Now she is also suffering from kidney stone problems.

updates.caption: Oct 16, 2012 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body:
Microcharity is supporting Nimal and her family by bearing a part of the medical expenses. Microcharity approved Rs. 10,000 for the year 2012. The plan is to raise Rs 10,000 for her in each quarter of the year and continuously support her in medical expenses.

Oct 18, 2012: MicroCharity raised Rs. 10,000 from 8 different individuals in 2 days. The fund is transferred to Nimal’s family.

updates.caption: January 29, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 1, 2013
updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well these days. She is now admitted at a hospital due to continuous nausea and vomiting. This is due to the hormone level issues she has been fighting with. The fund raised by MicroCharity was utilized to treat the hormone issue. She is also suffering from severe muscle strain. MicroCharity decided to continue supporting her by raising Rs. 10,000 for the first quarter of 2013.

Feb 4, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 6 different donors in 6 days.

updates.caption: April 30, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 2, 2013
updates.body:

May 2, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 5 different donations in 3 days.

updates.body:

Aug 21, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 10 different micro donations in 4 days.

updates.caption: Nov 13, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 4, 2013
updates.body:

Requesting all donors to please stand with us in this decision to increase our donation amount.
