title: Engineering fees for Adarsh
summary: Adarsh needs our help in continuing his education since his mother’s meager salary is not enough to support their family’s daily needs, as well as his education. Adarsh lives with his mother Ramani, 2 younger siblings, and his grandmother.

updates.caption: Sep 20, 2022 - Bangalore, Karnataka (MCID-111-22-23)
updates.body:
Adarsh needs our help in continuing his education since his mother’s meager salary is not enough to support their family’s daily needs, as well as his education. Adarsh lives with his mother Ramani, 2 younger siblings, and his grandmother.

Ramani is the only breadwinner of the family earning about Rs. 20,000 per month working at an architect’s office. But her income is not sufficient to finance her son’s education, who is currently doing Electronics and Communications Engineering in Bangalore.

They are currently staying at a rented house since their own house collapsed a while ago. They want to construct a new house in a small plot owned by them, but the family has no means to start construction.

They reached out to MicroCharity for financial assistance for Adarsh’s education. The total fee for the year comes to Rs. 90,000 of which they have arranged Rs.50,000 through friends and well-wishers.

updates.caption: Sep 20, 2022 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 40,000

updates.caption: Sep 30, 2022 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to the college.

title: Liver Transplant Surgery for Joseph Mathew
summary: Joseph has been suffering from chronic liver disease with portal hypertension for a long time. He was recently admitted to Amrita Hospital, Ernakulam, Kerala for further treatment. Doctors advised an emergency liver transplant, for which his wife has been identified as a suitable donor. The surgery which is a life-saving procedure is scheduled to be done in the next couple of weeks, but the family does not have enough money to fund this expensive transplant.

updates.caption: Sep 25, 2022 – Bangalore, Karnataka (MCID-113-22-23)
updates.body:
Joseph has been suffering from chronic liver disease with portal hypertension for a long time. He was recently admitted to Amrita Hospital, Ernakulam, Kerala for further treatment. Doctors advised an emergency liver transplant, for which his wife has been identified as a suitable donor. The surgery which is a life-saving procedure is scheduled to be done in the next couple of weeks, but the family does not have enough money to fund this expensive transplant.

Joseph was working as a chit fund agent, and his wife is a primary school teacher. Their children Alwin and Edwin are still at school. Joseph’s aged mother is also staying with them. Liver transplant surgery costs Rs. 22 lakhs; further follow-up and treatment would cost over Rs. 15,000 per month. They have no means to shell out this money, especially since Joseph and his wife would need complete rest during the coming months.

The family is reaching out to friends, well-wishers, and NGOs for help. MicroCharity is partnering with JCI Nalukody to help Joseph and his family during this difficult time in their lives. The fund collection is urgent since Joseph is admitted already, and the surgery is scheduled.

updates.caption: Sep 28, 2022 – Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 2,00,000

title: House construction for Usha Ashokan
summary: Usha and family have been staying in a rented house for a long time. They have 2.5 cents of land which they bought a few years back, but they were not able to start constructing the house. The family was recently allocated Rs. 2,80,000 for house construction through the Prime Minister's Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana. But they have no means to arrange the rest of the funds required for house construction.

updates.caption: Oct 10, 2022 - Thrissur, Kerala (MCID-114-22-23)
updates.body:
Usha and family have been staying in a rented house for a long time. They have 2.5 cents of land which they bought a few years back, but they were not able to start constructing the house. The family was recently allocated Rs. 2,80,000 for house construction through the Prime Minister’s Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana. But they have no means to arrange the rest of the funds required for house construction.

Usha is working as housekeeping staff. Her husband who was working as a daily wager is not able to work anymore after he recently had an accident. Their children are in school, and the family makes barely enough money to take care of their children’s education, as well as their daily expenses. They reached out to MicroCharity for financial assistance in house construction.

updates.caption: Oct 11, 2022 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 75,000

updates.caption: Sep 30, 2022 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to the Usha.

title: B.Com Fees for Martina
summary:
Martina has completed the first 3 semesters with Distinction and needs our support to pay the 2nd year fees.

Microcharity is raising Rs 35,000 to support her studies.

updates.caption: Jan 4, 2020 – Bangalore, Karnataka (MCID-117-19-20)
updates.body:
Martina from Bangalore, India needs our help in completing her PU course. Her father is a drunkard who does not look after the family. He comes home drunk late at night most days, causing a lot of nuisance to the family. Most of the days, they all have to go and seek shelter at a convent close by to escape the violence at home. Her mother, Elizabeth is working as a helper at a factory nearby earning INR 6000 per month.

Martina completed her 1st year PU with help from the nuns at the convent, and few other well-wishers. The nuns also advised her to move into a hostel so that she would not be troubled by her father’s behavior. Course fee for second year PU is INR 25,000 and hostel fee comes to INR 40,000. The family approached MicroCharity for financial assistance since they are not able to afford the college fee.

updates.caption: Jan 13, 2020 – Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 25,000. Fee is directly paid to College.

updates.caption: Feb 15, 2021 – College Fee for BCom (MCID-118-20-21)
updates.body:
Martina completed her PUC and has joined BCom. Her father is still not able to look after the family, and her mother’s meager income is not sufficient to fund her college fee.

College fee for 1st year BCom is INR 35000, of which MicroCharity is sponsoring INR 25,000.

updates.caption: Oct 20, 2022 – College Fee for 2nd Year(MCID-115-22-23)
updates.body:
Martina has completed the frist 3 semesters  with Distinction and needs our support to pay the 2nd year fees.

Microcharity is raising Rs 35,000 to support her studies.

updates.caption: Fund raising closed
updates.body:
Fund raising is closed. The fund will be transferred to Martina’s college.

title: Kidney Transplant for Sinomon
summary: Sinomon had been undergoing 3 dialysis sessions per week for past few years. Doctors have recommended kidney transplantation as the only solution. They have found a donor, and the transplantation surgery is scheduled for May 17th, 2022. The total cost for surgery and post-operative treatment is approximately Rs. 12 lakhs. Sinomon’s family is in dire need of financial assistance, since there are no other earning members.

updates.caption: May 1, 2021 - Idukki, Kerala (MCID-103-21-22)
updates.body:
Sinomon from Idukki, Kerala has been suffering from kidney failure for the past 5 years and is undergoing regular dialysis. Each dialysis costs about INR 1,000 and he need about 2 or 3 sessions a week. Kidney transplant is the only solution, but the family has no means to go ahead with it. 3 months back, while going for dialysis, Sinomon met with an accident and suffered a leg fracture. So currently he needs treatment for that as well.

He was working as a daily wage laborer, but currently he is not able to work after the leg injury. Currently, he is currently staying in a home for the destitute where they provide at least the basic meals. Sinomon’s mother goes for some government-sponsored daily wage work, which is the only income for the family. But that is not regular, and she hardly earns enough to sustain the family. They reached out to MicroCharity for help with the dialysis.

updates.caption: May 6, 2021 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 50,000.

updates.caption: May 6, 2022 - Fund Raising for Kidney Transplant (MCID-103-22-23)
updates.body:
Sinomon had been undergoing 3 dialysis sessions per week for past few years. Doctors have recommended kidney transplantation as the only solution. They have found a donor, and the transplantation surgery is scheduled for May 17th, 2022.

The total cost for surgery and post-operative treatment is approximately Rs. 12 lakhs. Sinomon’s family is in dire need of financial assistance, since there are no other earning members. 

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 1 lakh.

updates.caption: May 25, 2022 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Sinomon’s Hospital.

updates.caption: Nov 15, 2022 - Fund Raising for Post Transplant Treatment (MCID-117-22-23)
updates.body:
Sinomon’s kidney transplant was completed and he is recovering well. But he does not have any income since he is unable to work. Following the surgery, his medical expenses of Rs. 22,000 per month were covered by well-wishers. But he is now struggling to pay as the help is drying up, and he reached out to MicroCharity for help.

MicroCharity has approved fund request for Rs. 44,000 to help pay for the medication for the next two months.

updates.caption: Nov 30, 2022 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be paid against Sinomon’s Medical expenses.

title: College fee for Saniya
summary: Saniya's family is in dire need of our help, to start her BSc Nursing Course in Karnataka. Her family in Thrissur, Kerala consists of her father, mother, sister, grandfather, and grandmother living together in a small rented house. Her father is a union worker earning very little each month, her mother is a housewife, and her younger sister is a student. Saniya's grandmother is a cancer patient, and her treatment takes up a big part of the family's income.

updates.caption: Nov 15, 2022 - Thrissur, Kerala (MCID-117-22-23)
updates.body:
Saniya’s family is in dire need of our help, to start her BSc Nursing Course in Karnataka. Her family in Thrissur, Kerala consists of her father, mother, sister, grandfather, and grandmother living together in a small rented house.

Her father is a union worker earning very little each month, her mother is a housewife, and her younger sister is a student. Saniya’s grandmother is a cancer patient, and her treatment takes up a big part of the family’s income.

Saniya was expecting to arrange for an education loan, but it did not happen since the family had no assets to give to the bank as security. They already have a loan of Rs. 2 lakhs, which her father is struggling to repay. She reached out to MicroCharity for financial assistance with her college fee of Rs. 2,20,000 for the first year.

updates.caption: Nov 20, 2022 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 60,000

updates.caption: Dec 10, 2022 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Saniya’s college.

title: Medical Support for Nimal – December 2022
summary: Nimal has been paralyzed waist down for the past 19 years. She still has to go through many medical procedures and her family struggles to find the money for her treatment. MicroCharity has supported her with Rs.15,000 every quarter for the past 9 years, thanks to our kind-hearted donors.

updates.caption: Oct 1, 2012 - Kochuthovala, Kattappana, Kerala (MCID-111-12)
updates.body: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 10 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. The family is unable to repay the loans as they have to spend on the treatment. She had a tumour in uterus and she had to go through a hysterectomy. Now she is also suffering from kidney stone problems.

updates.caption: Oct 16, 2012 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body:
Microcharity is supporting Nimal and her family by bearing a part of the medical expenses. Microcharity approved Rs. 10,000 for the year 2012. The plan is to raise Rs 10,000 for her in each quarter of the year and continuously support her in medical expenses.

Oct 18, 2012: MicroCharity raised Rs. 10,000 from 8 different individuals in 2 days. The fund is transferred to Nimal’s family.

updates.caption: January 29, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 1, 2013
updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well these days. She is now admitted at a hospital due to continuous nausea and vomiting. This is due to the hormone level issues she has been fighting with. The fund raised by MicroCharity was utilized to treat the hormone issue. She is also suffering from severe muscle strain. MicroCharity decided to continue supporting her by raising Rs. 10,000 for the first quarter of 2013.

Feb 4, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 6 different donors in 6 days.

updates.caption: April 30, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 2, 2013
updates.body:
MicroCharity is continuing to support Nimal by raising Rs. 10,000 for the second quarter of 2013. Nimal is still not doing good and doctor’s have advised a thorough scan which will cost Rs. 25,000.

May 2, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 5 different donations in 3 days.

updates.caption: Aug 17, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 3, 2013
updates.body:
MicroCharity is continuing to support Nimal by raising Rs. 10,000 for the Third quarter of 2013. Nimal is still not doing good. Recently, her Calcium levels dropped very low and she is currently hospitalized at St Johns Hospital, Kattappana, Kerala .

Aug 21, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 10 different micro donations in 4 days.

updates.caption: Nov 13, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 4, 2013
updates.body:
MicroCharity is continuing to support Nimal by raising Rs. 10,000 for the fourth quarter of 2013.

Nov 15, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 6 different micro donations in 2 days.

updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well, and she was recently diagnosed with pituitary tumor and infection. In addition, she is also having problems due to Hiatal hernia. St. John’s Mission Hospital, Kattappana has sent her to Amrita Hospital for additional tests and check up.

The family is unable to keep up with all the medical expenses. MicroCharity approves an increased quarterly support amount of Rs. 50,000 this time, to help the family with the medical expenses.

Feb 6, 2014: Raised Rs. 50,000 from 23 micro donations in 3 days.

updates.caption: June 01, 2020 - Fund Raising for Quarter 3, 2020 (MCID-100-20-21)
updates.body:
Nimal has been paralyzed waist down for the past 18 years, as a result of a wrong injection administered to her in childhood. She has gone through a lot of hospitalizations and medical procedures; and her family is still struggling to fund her treatment. MicroCharity has been supporting her with Rs.10,000 every quarter or Rs. 40,000 every year. The family has always been extremely grateful for our support, and has never asked for more. But MicroCharity team felt that it would be good to increase the amount considering their increased expenses, to Rs.15,000 a quarter. Requesting all donors to please stand with us in this decision to increase our donation amount.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 15,000.

June 10,2020 – Fund raising closed for this cause. We have disbursed the amount to Nimal against her medical bills.

updates.caption: Sep 22, 2020 - Fund Raising for Quarter 4, 2020 (MCID-107-20-21)

MicroCharity continues to support Nimal, who has been paralyzed and bed-ridden for the past 18 years. The family is struggling with her medical expenses and frequent hospitalizations.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 15,000

Update  Sep 3,2020 : Fundraising closed for this cause. We will be disbursing money shortly to Nimal against here medical expenses.

updates.caption: Dec 07, 2020 - Fund Raising for Quarter 1, 2021 (MCID-114-20-21)
updates.body:
Nimal truly is a wonder, the way she has accepted her pain and sufferings over the last 18 years with maturity way beyond her years. She believes that God is with her, and with His strength, she stays strong and smiles through all her hardships.

MicroCharity is honored to continue supporting Nimal, who has been bedridden for the past 18 years. Listen to her story here, https://youtu.be/ZxetuOF6QPM (Malayalam video)

We continue to support the family every quarter, with help from our donors and well-wishers.

title: BPharm College fee for Anju
summary: Anju is in desperate need of financial assistance since her college will not let her appear for her BPharm second-semester exams if she has not cleared the pending fee payments. Her father is working as a driver, and her mother is a receptionist. The family earns just enough to manage their daily expenses.come.

updates.caption: Dec 15, 2022 - Bangalore, Karnataka (MCID-120-22-23)
updates.body:
Anju is in desperate need of financial assistance since her college will not let her appear for her BPharm second-semester exams if she has not cleared the pending fee payments. Her father is working as a driver, and her mother is a receptionist. The family earns just enough to manage their daily expenses.

They are not able to pay Anju’s college fee of Rs. 1.5 lakhs. They have paid Rs. 75,000 with help from friends and well-wishers. But now, the college wants her to clear all the fees due before giving her the hall tickets for exams. The family approached MicroCharity for urgent financial assistance.

updates.caption: Dec 20, 2022 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 75,000

updates.caption: Jan 10, 2023 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Anju’s College.

title: 10th standard fee for Ann Rose
summary: Ann Rose's father lost his job during Covid, closed down his business, abandoned his family, and stopped supporting them. Her mother is working as a nurse at a local hospital earning Rs. 25,000 per month which is not enough to take care of the expenses of 2 school-going daughters.

updates.caption: Jan 15, 2023 - Bangalore, Karnataka (MCID-122-22-23)
updates.body:
Ann Rose’s father lost his job during Covid, closed down his business, abandoned his family, and stopped supporting them. Her mother is working as a nurse at a local hospital earning Rs. 25,000 per month which is not enough to take care of the expenses of 2 school-going daughters.

Ann Rose is now in 10th grade, getting ready for the preparatory exams, but the family is not able to pay her yearly fee. They paid Rs. 12,000 of the total fee of Rs. 48,000, with help from friends and well-wishers. Her sister who is in 12th grade is able to continue her studies with help from nuns from the local convent.

The school will not allow Ann Rose to sit for the exams unless she clears the pending fee. The family approached MicroCharity for help in this urgent situation.

updates.caption: Jan 20, 2023 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 32,000

updates.caption: Jan 31, 2023 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Ann Rose’s School.

title: 10th standard fee for Premkumar
summary: Premkumar's father was working as an autorickshaw driver, before he was diagnosed with kidney failure. He is currently undergoing regular dialysis and is not able to work anymore. His mother is working as a housekeeping staff, but she is not able to earn enough to take care of her husband's treatment and the family's daily expenses.

updates.caption: Feb 5, 2023 - Bangalore, Karnataka (MCID-123-22-23)
updates.body:
Premkumar’s father was working as an autorickshaw driver, before he was diagnosed with kidney failure. He is currently undergoing regular dialysis and is not able to work anymore. His mother is working as a housekeeping staff, but she is not able to earn enough to take care of her husband’s treatment and the family’s daily expenses.

Premkumar is currently in 10th standard. The family has not been able to pay the school fees for a couple of years now, and the school will not allow him to attend his final exams without paying the due. The family needs to pay Rs. 1,00,000 as school fees now. So they approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: Feb 9, 2023 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 50,000

updates.caption: Jan 31, 2023 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Premkumar’s School.

title: House Construction for Robins John
summary: Robins Johns and his family lost their house and land completely in a landslide that happened in Vadakkemala, Idukki on 16th October 2020. His father got a major fracture on his leg. The bone was cut horizontally, and this has rendered him completely bedridden. His mother is a homemaker.

updates.caption: Feb 15, 2023 - Idukki, Kerala (MCID-124-22-23)
updates.body:
Robins Johns and his family lost their house and land completely in a landslide that happened in Vadakkemala, Idukki on 16th October 2021. His father got a major fracture on his leg. The bone was cut horizontally, and this has rendered him completely bedridden. His mother is a homemaker.

Robins recently joined a nearby school as menial staff, but since his government appointment papers are not ready, he is not given any salary. The family has no income and no house to live in. They have also not received any compensation money from the government.

The family is currently staying in a rented house with help from the local church. The church also gave them 3 cents of land for building a house. Some money was provided by a convent nearby to start house construction.

But the family has no means to carry on the house construction, since Robins has to take care of his father’s medical expenses as well as their household expenses. They approached MicroCharity for financial assistance to build a house.

updates.caption: Feb 19, 2023 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 80,000

title: Nursing college fee for Aneeta
summary: Aneeta needs our help in starting her BSc Nursing course. Her father is working as a driver earning Rs. 12,000 per month and her mother is working as a salesgirl in a shop earning Rs. 6,000 per month. Aneeta's sister is also studying for BSc. Nursing funded by an education loan.

updates.caption: Feb 15, 2023 - Ponkunnam, Kerala (MCID-125-22-23)
updates.body:
Aneeta needs our help in starting her BSc Nursing course. Her father is working as a driver earning Rs. 12,000 per month and her mother is working as a salesgirl in a shop earning Rs. 6,000 per month. Aneeta’s sister is also studying for BSc. Nursing funded by an education loan.

They have an education loan approved for Aneeta as well, but the family is not able to pay the initial contribution required from their end. The family is staying in a small house built on 4.5 cents of land, and even that has been mortgaged to fund their children’s education.

Aneeta’s admission process is completed at a college in Tamilnadu, but they are not able to pay the fee. So they approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: Mar 1, 2023 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 45,000

updates.caption: Mar 13, 2023 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Aneeta’s College.

But MicroCharity team felt that it would be good to increase the amount considering their increased expenses, to Rs.15,000 a quarter. Requesting all donors to please stand with us in this decision to increase our donation amount.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 15,000.

title: Nursing school fee for Anjitha
summary: Anjitha from Mundakkayam, Kerala is doing her 1st year BSc Nursing course in a Nursing school in Bangalore. Her father is a daily wage laborer and mother works as a contract employee, neither of them having regular work. Her sister Anjaly is a student. The family has barely enough income to sustain their daily expenses.

updates.caption: Jul 10, 2021 - Mundakkayam, Kerala (MCID-108-21-22)
updates.body:
Anjitha from Mundakkayam, Kerala is doing her 1st year BSc Nursing course in a Nursing school in Bangalore. Her father is a daily wage laborer and mother works as a contract employee, neither of them having regular work. Her sister Anjaly is a student. The family has barely enough income to sustain their daily expenses.

Anjitha joined college paying Rs.35,000, the bare minimum needed for admissions. Her 1st year exam is in August, and she needs to pay the full fee of Rs. 1.5 lakhs to attend the examinations.

The family is struggling to raise money during this time of the pandemic, when jobs are difficult to come by. They approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: Jul 21, 2021 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 75,000.

updates.caption: Aug 06, 2021 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Anjitha’s College.

updates.caption: Jul 21, 2021 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 75,000.

updates.caption: Mar 10, 2022 - Fund Raising Approved (MCID-128-21-22)
updates.body:
Anjitha completed her 1st year BSc. Nursing with good marks and has moved on to the 2nd year. Her college fee for this year is Rs. 1,25,000. Her family is not in a position to support her education, and they approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 65,000

updates.caption: Apr 20, 2023 - Fund Raising Approved (MCID-101-23-24)
updates.body:
Anjitha has completed her 2nd year B.Sc Nursing, scoring First class with 72%. She is requesting support to pay the 3rd year fee, since her father’s earnings from working as a daily wage laborer are not enough to support the children’s education.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 75,000

updates.caption: May 10, 2023 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Anjitha’s College.

title: Medical Support for Nimal – Quarter 3, 2023
summary: Nimal has been paralyzed waist down for the past 20 years. She still has to go through many medical procedures and her family struggles to find the money for her treatment. MicroCharity has supported her with Rs.15,000 every quarter for the past 20 years, thanks to our kind-hearted donors.

updates.caption: Oct 1, 2012 - Kochuthovala, Kattappana, Kerala (MCID-111-12)
updates.body: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 10 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. The family is unable to repay the loans as they have to spend on the treatment. She had a tumour in uterus and she had to go through a hysterectomy. Now she is also suffering from kidney stone problems.

updates.caption: Oct 16, 2012 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body:
Microcharity is supporting Nimal and her family by bearing a part of the medical expenses. Microcharity approved Rs. 10,000 for the year 2012. The plan is to raise Rs 10,000 for her in each quarter of the year and continuously support her in medical expenses.

Oct 18, 2012: MicroCharity raised Rs. 10,000 from 8 different individuals in 2 days. The fund is transferred to Nimal’s family.

updates.caption: January 29, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 1, 2013
updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well these days. She is now admitted at a hospital due to continuous nausea and vomiting. This is due to the hormone level issues she has been fighting with. The fund raised by MicroCharity was utilized to treat the hormone issue. She is also suffering from severe muscle strain. MicroCharity decided to continue supporting her by raising Rs. 10,000 for the first quarter of 2013.

Feb 4, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 6 different donors in 6 days.

updates.caption: April 30, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 2, 2013
updates.body:
MicroCharity is continuing to support Nimal by raising Rs. 10,000 for the second quarter of 2013. Nimal is still not doing good and doctor’s have advised a thorough scan which will cost Rs. 25,000.

May 2, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 5 different donations in 3 days.

updates.caption: Aug 17, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 3, 2013
updates.body:
MicroCharity is continuing to support Nimal by raising Rs. 10,000 for the Third quarter of 2013. Nimal is still not doing good. Recently, her Calcium levels dropped very low and she is currently hospitalized at St Johns Hospital, Kattappana, Kerala .

Aug 21, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 10 different micro donations in 4 days.

updates.caption: Nov 13, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 4, 2013

title: Kidney tumor treatment for Jijo
summary: Jijo M. O, a 44-year-old male from Kerala, India, is facing a critical health situation. Jijo was diagnosed with a kidney stone, but unfortunately, during the treatment, a tumor was discovered on his left kidney. The only viable solution is the removal of the affected kidney, which is essential for his survival.

updates.caption: Jun 10, 2023 - Ponkunnam, Kottayam, Kerala (MCID-103-23-24)
updates.body:
Jijo M. O, a 44-year-old male from Kerala, India, is facing a critical health situation. Jijo was diagnosed with a kidney stone, but unfortunately, during the treatment, a tumor was discovered on his left kidney. The only viable solution is the removal of the affected kidney, which is essential for his survival.

Jijo, a hardworking individual, was employed as a miscellaneous worker, earning a daily wage from various jobs. However, due to his deteriorating health, he is unable to continue working as instructed by his doctors. Jijo’s wife, Bindhumol, works under the Thozhilurappu scheme, but her income is very low. Their two children, Jerin and Jennifer, are both students. Jijo’s family is struggling to make ends meet and they do not have the money to pay for his medical treatment.

The estimated cost of Jijo’s treatment, including the surgery, follow-ups, laboratory tests, and subsequent cancer treatment, amounts to approximately Rs. 2,50,000 to Rs. 3,00,000. The family does not have any additional sources of income, and their limited savings have been depleted by the ongoing medical expenses. We humbly request your support to raise Rs. 75,000, which will significantly contribute to easing their financial burden and ensure Jijo receives the treatment he urgently needs.

Your kindness and generosity can make a remarkable difference in the life of Jijo and his family. Please consider making a donation, no matter how small, to help us reach our fundraising goal. Together, we can provide a ray of hope and enable Jijo to regain his health, while ensuring a brighter future for his dedicated wife and studious children.

Thank you for your compassion and support.

updates.caption: Jun 14, 2023 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 75,000.

updates.caption: Jun 30, 2023 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Jijo’s Hospital.

title: Nursing college fee for Athulya Benny
summary:
Athulya Benny and family is in need of our financial assistance in completing her nursing course. Her father is working in a flour mill earning Rs.10,000 per month. Her mother Jessy has many health issues and the family had to spend a lot of money for her treatment. She also has a younger brother who is in school now. Athulya is studying at Universal College of Nursing at an annual fee of Rs. 1,20,000. They managed to pay the admission fee of Rs. 30,000 through donations from friends and well-wishers, but has no means of raising the rest
of the amount. The family has other loans, and also has to manage the continuous medical expenses for her mother. They approached MicroCharity for financial support in paying the college fee.

updates.caption: May 1, 2022 - Fee for Second Year (MCID-102-22-23)
updates.body:

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 50,000

updates.caption: Jul 1, 2023 - Fee for Third Year (MCID-104-23-24)
updates.body:
We are excited to share the academic progress of Athulya. Athulya has successfully completed her second-year exams with a score of 67%. Despite facing financial hardships due to her mother’s health problems, Athulya’s determination and hard work have propelled her to the next stage of her studies.

However, Athulya’s family is still unable to support her education financially. As she enters the third year of her BSc Nursing program, we kindly request your assistance in raising Rs. 50,000 to cover her tuition fees. Your contribution will ensure that Athulya can continue her education without the burden of financial constraints, allowing her to focus on her studies and pursue her passion for nursing.

We sincerely appreciate your continued support and belief in the power of education to transform lives.

Thank you for your unwavering kindness and support.

MicroCharity approves fund request for Rs. 50,000.

updates.caption: Fundraising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed for this cause.      We will be transferring fee to Athulya’s college

title: Nursing education for Jhumuli Montry
summary: Jhumuli Montry is a young and ambitious girl facing financial hardships in pursuit of her dreams. She hails from a family in Odisha that has been going through a very difficult time due to the communal issues in Manipur and Odisha. Despite the challenges they have endured, Jhumuli's determination to pursue higher education remains unwavering.

updates.caption: Aug 01, 2023 - Bangalore, Karnataka(MCID-105-23-24)
updates.body:
Jhumuli Montry is a young and ambitious girl facing financial hardships in pursuit of her dreams. She hails from a family in Odisha that has been going through a very difficult time due to the civil unrest in Manipur and Odisha. Despite the challenges they have endured, Jhumuli’s determination to pursue higher education remains unwavering.

Her father works as a farmer, and her mother is a dedicated homemaker. With four children to care for and a modest total monthly income, their financial situation makes it difficult to support Jhumuli’s education. However, her dreams of becoming a nurse have led her to enroll in the General Nursing GNM course at St. Joseph’s College of Nursing, Mysuru.

The total yearly fee for Jhumuli’s nursing education amounts to Rs. 1,15,000, and her family has managed to gather Rs. 10,000 so far. To bridge the financial gap and ensure her continued education, we seek your support in raising some funds through MicroCharity.

Your contribution, no matter how small, can make a significant difference in Jhumuli’s life and open doors to a brighter future for her and her family. We kindly request your generous help to empower this bright student with the gift of education, enabling her to serve her community and fulfill her aspirations in the medical field.

Thank you for your compassion and support.

updates.caption: Aug 4, 2023 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 75,000.

updates.caption: Aug 30, 2023 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Jhumuli’s Hospital.

title: Medical Support for Nimal – Quarter 4, 2023
summary: Nimal has been paralyzed waist down for the past 20 years. She still has to go through many medical procedures and her family struggles to find the money for her treatment. MicroCharity has supported her with Rs.15,000 every quarter for the past 20 years, thanks to our kind-hearted donors.

updates.caption: Oct 1, 2012 - Kochuthovala, Kattappana, Kerala (MCID-111-12)
updates.body: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 10 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. The family is unable to repay the loans as they have to spend on the treatment. She had a tumour in uterus and she had to go through a hysterectomy. Now she is also suffering from kidney stone problems.

updates.caption: Oct 16, 2012 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body:
Microcharity is supporting Nimal and her family by bearing a part of the medical expenses. Microcharity approved Rs. 10,000 for the year 2012. The plan is to raise Rs 10,000 for her in each quarter of the year and continuously support her in medical expenses.

Oct 18, 2012: MicroCharity raised Rs. 10,000 from 8 different individuals in 2 days. The fund is transferred to Nimal’s family.

updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well these days. She is now admitted at a hospital due to continuous nausea and vomiting. This is due to the hormone level issues she has been fighting with. The fund raised by MicroCharity was utilized to treat the hormone issue. She is also suffering from severe muscle strain. MicroCharity decided to continue supporting her by raising Rs. 10,000 for the first quarter of 2013.

Feb 4, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 6 different donors in 6 days.

MicroCharity has been supporting her with Rs.10,000 every quarter or Rs. 40,000 every year. The family has always been extremely grateful for our support, and has never asked for more. But MicroCharity team felt that it would be good to increase the amount considering their increased expenses, to Rs.15,000 a quarter. Requesting all donors to please stand with us in this decision to increase our donation amount.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 15,000.

June 10,2020 – Fund raising closed for this cause. We have disbursed the amount to Nimal against her medical bills.

title: Medical Support for Nimal – Quarter 1, 2024
summary: Nimal has been paralyzed waist down for the past 20 years. She still has to go through many medical procedures and her family struggles to find the money for her treatment. MicroCharity has supported her with Rs.15,000 every quarter for the past 20 years, thanks to our kind-hearted donors.

updates.caption: Oct 1, 2012 - Kochuthovala, Kattappana, Kerala (MCID-111-12)
updates.body: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 10 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. The family is unable to repay the loans as they have to spend on the treatment. She had a tumour in uterus and she had to go through a hysterectomy. Now she is also suffering from kidney stone problems.

updates.caption: Oct 16, 2012 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body:
Microcharity is supporting Nimal and her family by bearing a part of the medical expenses. Microcharity approved Rs. 10,000 for the year 2012. The plan is to raise Rs 10,000 for her in each quarter of the year and continuously support her in medical expenses.

Oct 18, 2012: MicroCharity raised Rs. 10,000 from 8 different individuals in 2 days. The fund is transferred to Nimal’s family.

updates.caption: January 29, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 1, 2013
updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well these days. She is now admitted at a hospital due to continuous nausea and vomiting. This is due to the hormone level issues she has been fighting with. The fund raised by MicroCharity was utilized to treat the hormone issue. She is also suffering from severe muscle strain. MicroCharity decided to continue supporting her by raising Rs. 10,000 for the first quarter of 2013.

Feb 4, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 6 different donors in 6 days.

updates.caption: April 30, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 2, 2013
updates.body:

May 2, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 5 different donations in 3 days.

updates.caption: Aug 17, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 3, 2013
updates.body:

Aug 21, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 10 different micro donations in 4 days.

The family is unable to keep up with all the medical expenses. MicroCharity approves an increased quarterly support amount of Rs. 50,000 this time, to help the family with the medical expenses.

title: Medical treatment for Fathima Bee
summary: Fathima Bee from Bihar has suffered 5 miscarriages already. Currently she is 3 months pregnant and suffering from Immune thrombocytopenic purpura (ITP), is a blood disorder involving a decrease in the number of platelets in the blood. She started bleeding recently and was admitted to St. John’s hospital, Bangalore.

updates.caption: Feb 01, 2024 - Bangalore, Karnataka(MCID-108-23-24)
updates.body:
Fathima Bee from Bihar has suffered 5 miscarriages already. Currently she is 3 months pregnant and suffering from Immune thrombocytopenic purpura (ITP), is a blood disorder involving a decrease in the number of platelets in the blood. She started bleeding recently and was admitted to St. John’s hospital, Bangalore.

She needs IVIG injection immediately to save her life as well as that of her unborn child. Fathima Bee’s husband is working as a daily labourer and he is the only breadwinner of the family. The first dose of IVIG injection has to be given immediately to prevent further bleeding and a drop in platelet count.

One dose costs Rs. 96,000, and she might need one more dose later, after evaluating the condition post the first dose. They reached out to MicroCharity for financial assistance since the family has no means to pay even for one dose.

updates.caption: Feb 5, 2024 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 96,000.

updates.caption: Feb 28, 2024 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Fathima’s Hospital.

title: Nursing School Fee for Tincy
summary: Tincy needs our support to complete her nursing course and after that, to provide for her family. She lives in Thamarassery with her parents and a younger brother. Her father is working as a coolie at Rs. 5000 per month, and does not have regular work. Her mother is not working, and her younger brother is still in school.

updates.caption: Nov 1, 2021 - Thamarassery, Kerala (MCID-118-21-22)
updates.body:
Tincy needs our support to complete her nursing course and after that, to provide for her family. She lives in Thamarassery with her parents and a younger brother. Her father is working as a coolie at Rs. 5000 per month, and does not have regular work. Her mother is not working, and her younger brother is still in school.

The family stays in a small house with an asbestos roof. Tincy’s father is not in a position to support the family’s daily expenses and both the children’s education. Tincy is good at studies and wants to complete her education, so that she can take care of her family.

She got admissions at a Nursing college in Thalassery, Kerala. The total fee for this academic year comes to Rs. 76,000 and the family has no way to fund this. So they approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: MicroCharity approves fund request!
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 25,000.

updates.caption: Feb 15, 2024 - Fee for Second Year (MCID-111-23-24)
updates.body: Tincy has passed the First year of the course and has moved on to the second year. Her family’s financial condition has not improved much. She requested financial assistance this year again. MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 25,000.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Tincy’s college.

title: House Construction for Yamuna
summary: Yamuna and family are in dire need of our help. She has been battling kidney disease for the past 10 years, and now that has escalated to the point where her kidneys have begun to fail, affecting other vital organs like her liver and heart. Yamuna’s husband, the sole breadwinner of their family has cardiac health issues, rendering him unable to work regularly.

updates.caption: Feb 20, 2024 - Kottayam, Kerala(MCID-112-23-24)
updates.body:
Yamuna and family are in dire need of our help. She has been battling kidney disease for the past 10 years, and now that has escalated to the point where her kidneys have begun to fail, affecting other vital organs like her liver and heart. Yamuna’s husband, the sole breadwinner of their family has cardiac health issues, rendering him unable to work regularly.

Her husband was working as a helper at a restaurant, but he is not able to continue anymore. They have 2 daughters, the elder on studying General Nursing and the younger on studying ITI. The family has no home of their own, and are staying in a rented house.

Yamuna’s school alumni group and well-wishers are pitching in with collection drives to help them build a house and also to support the medical expenses.

Total cost for building a house with be close to 10 lakhs. MicroCharity is joining hands in helping this family move in to their own home, so that they can save on rent to focus on medical expenses and children’s education.

updates.caption: Feb 25, 2024 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 60,000.

updates.caption: Mar 15, 2024 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred against construction expenses.

title: Nursing school fee for Anjitha

Anjitha joined college paying Rs.35,000, the bare minimum needed for admissions. Her 1st year exam is in August, and she needs to pay the full fee of Rs. 1.5 lakhs to attend the examinations.

The family is struggling to raise money during this time of the pandemic, when jobs are difficult to come by. They approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

title: Medical Support for Nimal – Quarter 2, 2024
summary: Nimal has been paralyzed waist down for the past 20 years. She still has to go through many medical procedures and her family struggles to find the money for her treatment. MicroCharity has supported her with Rs.15,000 every quarter for the past 20 years, thanks to our kind-hearted donors.

updates.caption: Oct 1, 2012 - Kochuthovala, Kattappana, Kerala (MCID-111-12)
updates.body: Nimal is paralysed from waist down. The tragedy hit her about 10 years ago when she was studying in 12th grade. She had a fever and during the treatment the doctor administered a wrong injection which made her paralyzed from waist down. A hip replacement surgery was done to make her walk, but that made her condition worse. They have very little land which is mortgaged for her treatment. The family is unable to repay the loans as they have to spend on the treatment. She had a tumour in uterus and she had to go through a hysterectomy. Now she is also suffering from kidney stone problems.

updates.caption: Oct 16, 2012 - MicroCharity approves the fund request
updates.body:
Microcharity is supporting Nimal and her family by bearing a part of the medical expenses. Microcharity approved Rs. 10,000 for the year 2012. The plan is to raise Rs 10,000 for her in each quarter of the year and continuously support her in medical expenses.

Oct 18, 2012: MicroCharity raised Rs. 10,000 from 8 different individuals in 2 days. The fund is transferred to Nimal’s family.

updates.caption: January 29, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 1, 2013
updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well these days. She is now admitted at a hospital due to continuous nausea and vomiting. This is due to the hormone level issues she has been fighting with. The fund raised by MicroCharity was utilized to treat the hormone issue. She is also suffering from severe muscle strain. MicroCharity decided to continue supporting her by raising Rs. 10,000 for the first quarter of 2013.

Feb 4, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 6 different donors in 6 days.

updates.caption: April 30, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 2, 2013
updates.body:
MicroCharity is continuing to support Nimal by raising Rs. 10,000 for the second quarter of 2013. Nimal is still not doing good and doctor’s have advised a thorough scan which will cost Rs. 25,000.

May 2, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 5 different donations in 3 days.

updates.caption: Aug 17, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 3, 2013
updates.body:
MicroCharity is continuing to support Nimal by raising Rs. 10,000 for the Third quarter of 2013. Nimal is still not doing good. Recently, her Calcium levels dropped very low and she is currently hospitalized at St Johns Hospital, Kattappana, Kerala .

Aug 21, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 10 different micro donations in 4 days.

updates.caption: Nov 13, 2013 - Fund raising for Quarter 4, 2013
updates.body:
MicroCharity is continuing to support Nimal by raising Rs. 10,000 for the fourth quarter of 2013.

Nov 15, 2013: Raised Rs. 10,000 from 6 different micro donations in 2 days.

updates.caption: Feb 1, 2014 - Fund raising for Quarter 1, 2014
updates.body:
Nimal has not been keeping well, and she was recently diagnosed with pituitary tumor and infection. In addition, she is also having problems due to Hiatal hernia. St. John’s Mission Hospital, Kattappana has sent her to Amrita Hospital for additional tests and check up.

The family is unable to keep up with all the medical expenses. MicroCharity approves an increased quarterly support amount of Rs. 50,000 this time, to help the family with the medical expenses.

Feb 6, 2014: Raised Rs. 50,000 from 23 micro donations in 3 days.

updates.caption: Apr 15, 2014 - Fund Raising for Quarter 2, 2014 (MCID-103-14-15)

But MicroCharity team felt that it would be good to increase the amount considering their increased expenses, to Rs.15,000 a quarter. title: Nursing College Fee for Treesa Mariya Jose
summary: Treesa needs our help in completing her BSc Nursing Course. She lost her father when she was very young. And her mother is a daily wage labourer working very hard to make ends meet.

updates.caption: Apr 1, 2024 - Elanji, Kerala(MCID-101-24-25)
updates.body:
Treesa needs our help in completing her BSc Nursing Course. She lost her father when she was very young. And her mother is a daily wage labourer working very hard to make ends meet.

She stays with her mother, younger brother, and grandfather in their own house, but they already have some loans for friends. Treesa completed her 2nd year successfully and is soon starting 3rd year of her BSc Nursing Course.

The total fee for the year is Rs. 1,34,000. She reached out to MicroCharity for financial assistance since they are not in a position to pay the amount.

updates.caption: Apr 1, 2024 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 80,000.

updates.caption: Apr 15, 2024 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Treesa’s College.

title: College fee for Saniya
summary: Saniya's family is in dire need of our help, to start her BSc Nursing Course in Karnataka. Her family in Thrissur, Kerala consists of her father, mother, sister, grandfather, and grandmother living together in a small rented house. Her father is a union worker earning very little each month, her mother is a housewife, and her younger sister is a student. Saniya's grandmother is a cancer patient, and her treatment takes up a big part of the family's income.

updates.caption: Nov 15, 2022 - Thrissur, Kerala (MCID-118-22-23)
updates.body:
Saniya’s family is in dire need of our help, to start her BSc Nursing Course in Karnataka. Her family in Thrissur, Kerala consists of her father, mother, sister, grandfather, and grandmother living together in a small rented house.

Her father is a union worker earning very little each month, her mother is a housewife, and her younger sister is a student. Saniya’s grandmother is a cancer patient, and her treatment takes up a big part of the family’s income.

Saniya was expecting to arrange for an education loan, but it did not happen since the family had no assets to give to the bank as security. They already have a loan of Rs. 2 lakhs, which her father is struggling to repay. She reached out to MicroCharity for financial assistance with her college fee of Rs. 2,20,000 for the first year.

updates.caption: Nov 20, 2022 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 60,000

updates.caption: Jun 20, 2024 - Fund Raising for Second Year (MCID-102-24-25)
updates.body:
Saniya has completed her first year Nursing course. She had a fall and fractured her back last year, due to which she could not attend many classes.

Despite that, she studied well and cleared all her papers except one. She has promised to study harder this year to clear all the subjects, and score well.

updates.caption: Jun 28, 2024 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 60,000

updates.caption: Jul 10, 2024 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Saniya’s college.

title: Kidney Transplant for Pareemon
summary:
Pareemon NB is a dedicated first-generation entrepreneur whose passion for his business has been a driving force in his life. Unfortunately, he has recently been diagnosed with chronic kidney disease and requires a transplant to return to good health.

Hailing from a lower-middle-class family, Pareemon supports his wife and three children. His treatment currently involves three dialysis sessions per week until he can undergo the transplant.

With your support, we can help Pareemon regain his health and return to his business, restoring stability to his family's life. Your contribution will make a significant difference in his journey towards recovery.

Thank you for considering a donation to help Pareemon NB in this critical time.

updates.caption: Aug 2, 2024 - Ernakulam, Kerala (MCID-103-24-25)
updates.body:

title: Help Thomas build a House
summary: Thomas Sebastian, a 64-year-old auto driver from Nalukody, Kottayam, needs our help to build a foundation for his house. He works as an auto driver, earning Rs. 10,000 per month. His wife is a homemaker, and his two sons work as daily laborers doing welding work. They live in an old house on a small plot of land.

updates.caption: Aug 20, 2024 - Kottayam, Kerala (MCID-104-24-25)
updates.body:
Thomas Sebastian, a 64-year-old auto driver from Nalukody, Kottayam needs our help in building a foundation for his house. He is working as an auto driver earning Rs. 10,000 per month. His wife is a homemaker and his two sons are working as daily labourers doing welding work. They live in an old house on a small plot of land.

The family has been allotted a 450 sq ft house under a government scheme, which offers hope for a better future. However, to claim the first installment of the grant, Thomas must first demolish his old house and build the foundation for the new one.

The family is in desperate need of money to start this crucial phase of construction, which would cost Rs. 2,00,000. With no savings and existing loans of about 2 lakhs, they are turning to the generosity of kind-hearted individuals.

Your contribution can help Thomas and his family lay the foundation for a new home, providing them with a safe and secure place to live. Any amount you can donate will make a significant difference in their lives and help them achieve the stability they so desperately need.

updates.caption: Aug 21, 2024 - Fund Request Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 50,000.

updates.caption: Aug 28, 2024 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The funds will be used for Thomas’s house construction

updates.caption: Sep 10, 2024 - Pala, Kerala (MCID-105-24-25)
updates.body:
Catherin Anna Roby needs our help in completing her Nursing degree from Mar Sleeva College of Nursing, Pala. MicroCharity had helped her dad, Roby with his liver transplant surgery [link to https://www.microcharity.com/donations/liver-transplant-surgery-for-roby/] in 2020. The only earning member of Catherin’s family is her mother who is working as a lab technician at Rs. 22,000 per month. Her elderly grandmother and younger sister, who is a student, also stays with them.

Catherin’s dream of becoming a healthcare professional faces financial hurdles, as her family is struggling to meet the annual fee of ₹1,56,325. They approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

title: Kidney Transplant for Madhu
summary: Madhu is a devoted husband and father of two, but for the past two years, he has been battling severe kidney disease. Both of his kidneys have failed, leaving him reliant on dialysis three times a week. Each session is a reminder of the urgency of his situation—he needs a kidney transplant as soon as possible to survive. Fortunately, Madhu's wife has courageously stepped forward to donate one of her kidneys, but they still face significant costs associated with the transplant.

updates.caption: Oct 15, 2024 - Kattappana, Kerala (MCID-106-24-25)
updates.body:
Madhu is a devoted husband and father of two, but for the past two years, he has been battling severe kidney disease. Both of his kidneys have failed, leaving him reliant on dialysis three times a week. Each session is a reminder of the urgency of his situation—he needs a kidney transplant as soon as possible to survive. Fortunately, Madhu’s wife has courageously stepped forward to donate one of her kidneys, but they still face significant costs associated with the transplant.

The cost of the kidney transplant is a staggering INR 15 lakhs. Madhu and his family have already exhausted their savings on treatment, and they now find themselves in a desperate financial crisis. His family consists of his loving wife, two young children, and his elderly mother. Their only asset is a small house on 5 cents of land, which they hold on to tightly as their last refuge.

Madhu’s situation is critical, and time is running out. The burden of medical expenses has overwhelmed his family, and they are reaching out for help. Your contribution can make a significant difference in Madhu’s life and give him a fighting chance at recovery.

Every contribution, no matter how small, brings us closer to the goal. Your generosity will directly support Madhu’s transplant and medical care. Please share Madhu’s story with your friends, family, and social networks. The more people who know about his situation, the greater our chances of reaching our target.

Madhu’s life hangs in the balance, and he urgently needs your support. Together, we can help him get the treatment he desperately needs to live a healthy life with his family. Thank you for your kindness and compassion. Every bit helps, and with your support, we can give Madhu the chance he deserves to thrive and be with his loved ones.

updates.caption: Oct 18, 2024 - Fund Request Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves the fund request.

updates.caption: Nov 4, 2024 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Madhu’s Hospital.

title: Nursing School Fee for Tincy
summary: Tincy needs our support to complete her nursing course and after that, to provide for her family. She lives in Thamarassery with her parents and a younger brother. Her father is working as a coolie at Rs. 5000 per month, and does not have regular work. Her mother is not working, and her younger brother is still in school.

updates.caption: Nov 1, 2021 - Thamarassery, Kerala (MCID-118-21-22)
updates.body:
Tincy needs our support to complete her nursing course and after that, to provide for her family. She lives in Thamarassery with her parents and a younger brother. Her father is working as a coolie at Rs. 5000 per month, and does not have regular work. Her mother is not working, and her younger brother is still in school.

The family stays in a small house with an asbestos roof. Tincy’s father is not in a position to support the family’s daily expenses and both the children’s education. Tincy is good at studies and wants to complete her education, so that she can take care of her family.

She got admissions at a Nursing college in Thalassery, Kerala. The total fee for this academic year comes to Rs. 76,000 and the family has no way to fund this. So they approached MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: MicroCharity approves fund request!
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 25,000.

updates.caption: Feb 15, 2024 - Fee for Second Year (MCID-111-23-24)
updates.body: Tincy has passed the First year of the course and has moved on to the second year. Her family’s financial condition has not improved much. She requested financial assistance this year again. MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 25,000.

updates.caption: Feb 12, 2025 - Fee for Third Year (MCID-107-24-25)
updates.body: Tincy has passed the second year of the course and has moved on to the third year. She requested financial assistance this year again. MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 25,000.

updates.caption: Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Tincy’s college.

title: College fee for Saniya
summary: Saniya's family is in dire need of our help, to start her BSc Nursing Course in Karnataka. Her family in Thrissur, Kerala consists of her father, mother, sister, grandfather, and grandmother living together in a small rented house. Her father is a union worker earning very little each month, her mother is a housewife, and her younger sister is a student. Saniya's grandmother is a cancer patient, and her treatment takes up a big part of the family's income.

updates.caption: Nov 15, 2022 - Thrissur, Kerala (MCID-118-22-23)
updates.body:
Saniya’s family is in dire need of our help, to start her BSc Nursing Course in Karnataka. Her family in Thrissur, Kerala consists of her father, mother, sister, grandfather, and grandmother living together in a small rented house.

Her father is a union worker earning very little each month, her mother is a housewife, and her younger sister is a student. Saniya’s grandmother is a cancer patient, and her treatment takes up a big part of the family’s income.

Saniya was expecting to arrange for an education loan, but it did not happen since the family had no assets to give to the bank as security. They already have a loan of Rs. 2 lakhs, which her father is struggling to repay. She reached out to MicroCharity for financial assistance with her college fee of Rs. 2,20,000 for the first year.

updates.caption: Nov 20, 2022 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 60,000

updates.caption: Jun 20, 2024 - Fund Raising for Second Year (MCID-102-24-25)
updates.body:
Saniya has completed her first year Nursing course. She had a fall and fractured her back last year, due to which she could not attend many classes.

Despite that, she studied well and cleared all her papers except one. She has promised to study harder this year to clear all the subjects, and score well.

updates.caption: Jun 28, 2024 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 60,000

updates.caption: Mar 15, 2025 - Fund Raising for Third Year (MCID-107-24-25)
updates.body: Saniya achieved an impressive 65% in her second-year examinations, successfully meeting the academic requirements to advance to the third year of her studies. We are incredibly proud of her accomplishment and will continue to offer our full support as she takes on the challenges of her next academic year.

updates.caption: Mar 17, 2025 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 60,000

updates.caption: Apr 1, 2025 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Saniya’s college.

His father's income is not enough to meet the household expenses and also fund the children's education. The family has no other savings or assets. So they reached out to MicroCharity for financial assistance.

updates.caption: Jul 20, 2025 - Kannur, Kerala (MCID-101-25-26)
updates.body: Christo Vinoy from Kelakam, Kannur, needs our help in completing his course in General Nursing. He is doing his 3rd year at Rajeev School of Nursing, Hassan, Karnataka and the fee for this year comes to Rs. 85,000.He stays with his father who is a famer, earning about Rs. 15,000 per month; his mother who is a homemaker, his sister Christy who is working as a nurse, and younger brother Amal who is in 12th.His father’s income is not enough to meet the household expenses and also fund the children’s education. The family has no other savings or assets. So they reached out to MicroCharity for financial assistance.

title: Nursing education for Jhumuli Montry
summary: Jhumuli Montry is a young and ambitious girl facing financial hardships in pursuit of her dreams. She hails from a family in Odisha that has been going through a very difficult time due to the communal issues in Manipur and Odisha. Despite the challenges they have endured, Jhumuli's determination to pursue higher education remains unwavering.

updates.caption: Aug 01, 2023 - Bangalore, Karnataka(MCID-105-23-24)
updates.body:
Jhumuli Montry is a young and ambitious girl facing financial hardships in pursuit of her dreams. She hails from a family in Odisha that has been going through a very difficult time due to the civil unrest in Manipur and Odisha. Despite the challenges they have endured, Jhumuli’s determination to pursue higher education remains unwavering.

Her father works as a farmer, and her mother is a dedicated homemaker. With four children to care for and a modest total monthly income, their financial situation makes it difficult to support Jhumuli’s education. However, her dreams of becoming a nurse have led her to enroll in the General Nursing GNM course at St. Joseph’s College of Nursing, Mysuru.

The total yearly fee for Jhumuli’s nursing education amounts to Rs. 1,15,000, and her family has managed to gather Rs. 10,000 so far. To bridge the financial gap and ensure her continued education, we seek your support in raising some funds through MicroCharity.

Your contribution, no matter how small, can make a significant difference in Jhumuli’s life and open doors to a brighter future for her and her family. We kindly request your generous help to empower this bright student with the gift of education, enabling her to serve her community and fulfill her aspirations in the medical field.

Thank you for your compassion and support.

updates.caption: Aug 4, 2023 - Fund Raising Approved
updates.body: MicroCharity approves fund request of INR 75,000.

updates.caption: Sep 18, 2025 - Fund Raising Approved (MCID-102-25-26)
updates.body:
Jhumuli completed her first year General Nursing in Feb 2025 with first class, and has started her second year of college. 

Fee for the current year is Rs. 1 lakh. Her father and mother have no means to support her education, and they reached out to MicroCharity for support.

MicroCharity approves fund request of Rs. 1 lakh.

updates.caption: Sep 30, 2025 - Fund Raising Closed
updates.body: Fund raising closed. The fund will be transferred to Jhumuli’s College.
